Kronos uses the adamantine sickle given by Gaia to castrate Uranus; from the blood and severed organ are born the Erinyes, the Giants (Gigantes), and Aphrodite (from sea-foam).
Kronos swallows each of his children at birth to prevent the prophecy of his overthrow. Tricked by Rhea who substitutes a stone for Zeus.
Zeus, raised in secret, returns to free his siblings from Kronos's belly and leads them in the Titanomachy. Kronos is overthrown. The IE three-generation succession-pattern (Uranus → Kronos → Zeus).
Iapetus is father of Prometheus (who creates / champions humanity), Epimetheus, Atlas, and Menoetius. The Iapetus-Japheth name parallel is old; debated but structurally suggestive.
After the Titanomachy, Atlas is condemned to hold up the heavens forever. Structural parallel to the four Quinametzin sky-bearers and the Hindu Lokapalas.
Rhea bears the elder Olympians and saves Zeus from Kronos by substitution.
The Jötnar predate the gods (Ymir is first being); the Æsir descend partly from them; they rival the Æsir continuously and destroy the world at Ragnarök.
The Jötnar dwell in Jötunheim, the realm of giants. They are associated with frost, mountains, and the wild edges of the cultivated cosmos.

facets.facet_name: Destroyers of the World at Ragnarök
facets.core_claim: At Ragnarök the Jötnar, led by Surtr, cross Bifrost and destroy the cosmos with fire. The eschatological inversion of the Titanomachy — instead of being defeated by the gods, the giants finally win at the end.
Ymir is first being. Killed by Odin, Vili, and Vé; flesh becomes earth, blood becomes sea, skull becomes sky, brain becomes clouds, eyebrows fence off Midgard. The Norse direct reflex of PIE *Yemo.
Old Norse Ymir descends from PIE *Yemo 'twin' — direct cognate with Vedic Yama, Avestan Yima, Latin geminus. The same PIE deity-name preserved across the IE family.
Surtr leads the Jötnar across Bifrost at Ragnarök and burns the cosmos with his flaming sword. The cosmic-fire-ender. Parallel to Kalki the eschatological rider.
Bestla, a jötunn, marries Borr and bears Odin, Vili, and Vé. The Norse Æsir are therefore half-jötunn by maternal descent.

facets.facet_name: Jötunn Blood in the Æsir Line
facets.core_claim: The fact that the chief Norse god has a jötunn mother — and that Loki, who has jötunn parentage, lives among the Æsir — makes the divine-jötunn boundary porous. Structurally similar to the bnei-elohim / daughters-of-men interbreeding of Gen 6, with opposite valence (in Norse it is the norm, not the cataclysm).
Thrym hides Mjölnir eight miles underground and demands Freyja as bride; Thor disguises himself as Freyja and recovers the hammer, killing Thrym.

facets.facet_name: Jötunn Who Marries into the Æsir
facets.core_claim: Skadi, daughter of the jötunn Þjazi, marries Njörðr after her father's death — formalizing alliance between Æsir and jötnar.

facets.facet_name: Eschatological Horseman at the End of the Kali Yuga
facets.core_claim: Kalki, the tenth avatar of Vishnu, will appear at the end of the Kali Yuga on a white horse, with a flaming sword, to destroy the wicked and inaugurate a new age. Direct parallel to the Christian Parousia (Rev 19's rider on the white horse) and to Surtr in Norse Ragnarök.
Adi-Buddha is the primordial Buddha — the ground of being from which all Buddhas and phenomena arise.
Mara tries to prevent the Buddha's awakening with armies, three daughters (Desire/Discontent/Craving), and accusations. Defeated by the earth-touching gesture.

facets.facet_name: Lord of the Realm of Becoming
facets.core_claim: Mara rules the highest desire-realm heaven — the most refined samsaric trap, where conditioned happiness is mistaken for liberation.
Avalokitesvara hears all cries and comes to aid — thousand arms, thousand eyes, reaching everywhere simultaneously.
Maitreya waits in Tushita heaven and will descend to re-establish dharma — the Buddhist returning-savior figure.
Tara is the swift-acting feminine savior, born from Avalokitesvara's tears of compassion for all beings.
Tara is 'mother of all Buddhas' — Prajnaparamita (perfect wisdom) personified as the womb from which all awakening is born.
Zurvan is Time-Eternity — the impersonal principle above and prior to both Ahura Mazda and Angra Mainyu.
Ahura Mazda is the supreme uncreated source of all good, light, and truth.
Ahura Mazda is the source of asha — cosmic truth-order. Cognate of Vedic rta and Egyptian Ma'at.
Angra Mainyu chose evil from the beginning and attacked Ahura Mazda's good creation — introducing death, disease, and falsehood.
Zoroastrian cosmic dualism is the oldest fully developed adversary theology — directly influencing Second Temple Judaism on Satan and evil.
Saoshyant will be born of a virgin from Zarathustra's preserved seed, defeat Angra Mainyu, resurrect the dead.
The Saoshyant's virgin birth is the earliest documented version of the virgin-birth savior motif — predating the Christian nativity by 600+ years.
The Jade Emperor rules the celestial bureaucracy — Chinese cosmos-as-empire.
The Jade Emperor governs through delegated divine officials — the most bureaucratic divine order in world religion.
Laozi wrote the Tao Te Ching at the mountain pass before departing westward — the minimal act of descending wisdom to the human world.
In Taoist religion, Laozi is deified as Taishang Laojun — the highest of the Three Pure Ones, the Tao itself made manifest.
Nüwa fashioned the first humans from yellow earth by hand, then trailed a vine through mud so drops became common people.
When the pillar of heaven broke, Nüwa smelted five-colored stones to patch the sky, stabilized the four corners, and slew the flood-dragon.
Olodumare is beyond worship and direct approach — the ultimate source who delegates everything to the orishas.
Olodumare delegates all cosmic governance to the 401 orishas, then withdraws.
Olodumare assigned Obatala to fashion human bodies from clay while Olodumare breathes in the soul.

facets.facet_name: Orisha of Purity and White Cloth
facets.core_claim: Obatala represents the highest moral principles among the orishas — purity, patience, wisdom, justice.
Eshu mediates between humans and orishas, must be propitiated first. Neither good nor evil — the principle of radical indeterminacy.
Eshu lives at all crossroads — physical and metaphysical. No journey begins without his permission.

facets.facet_name: Sweet-Water Goddess of Love and Fertility
facets.core_claim: Oshun rules sweet fresh waters and the sweetness of life: love, beauty, fertility, art, abundance.
Oshun's apparent gentleness conceals absolute necessity — the world collapsed without her blessing.
Viracocha emerges from Lake Titicaca, creates the cosmos, civilizes humanity, and departs — sometimes prophesied to return.
After creating the world, Viracocha walked among humanity as a wandering bearded teacher before departing westward across the Pacific.
Pachamama IS the earth — mountains, fields, rivers. Offerings are made by returning gifts to the earth that gave them.
Tangaroa rules the ocean — the vast Pacific, source of life and destroyer.
In Samoan tradition, Tagaloa was the only being in the void — created the universe by thought alone.
Maui is the quintessential Polynesian trickster-hero — fished up islands, lassoed the sun, found fire, defied death.

facets.facet_name: The Culture Hero Who Shaped the World
facets.core_claim: Maui's acts gave humanity the conditions for civilization: land, time (slowing the sun), and fire. He died attempting the final gift — immortality.
Pele creates and destroys in a single continuous act — the newest and most ancient landscape, continuously making and remaking.
Pele's volcanic eruptions express divine passion — her love affairs and jealousies manifest as lava flows.
Perun rules the upper world — sky, thunder, rain. His axe and lightning bolts uphold cosmic order against Veles.
Perun eternally battles Veles the serpent-dragon in the Slavic preservation of the PIE storm-god vs cosmic-serpent dyad.
Veles rules Nav (the realm of the dead). He steals Perun's cattle, provoking the eternal divine battle that produces thunder and rain.

facets.facet_name: Lord of Wealth, Cattle, and Magic
facets.core_claim: Veles is also the patron of cattle, commerce, music, poetry, and magic — the ambivalent shadow twin of Perun's solar order.
Mokosh spins the thread of fate at night — entering homes to spin alongside women.
Mokosh is the wet, fertile earth itself — 'moist mother earth'. She receives the dead and gives birth to the living.
Amaterasu is the sun-goddess, supreme kami, and ancestress of the Japanese imperial line.

facets.facet_name: Hidden in Cave, Restored by the Gods
facets.core_claim: Amaterasu's withdrawal plunged the world into darkness; her restoration by the gods' laughter re-established cosmic order.
Susanoo is the storm-kami, exiled from heaven for outrages against Amaterasu.
Susanoo slays Yamata-no-Orochi by intoxicating it with sake; finds the Kusanagi sword in its tail.
Izanami becomes queen of Yomi — vows to kill a thousand people per day after Izanagi abandons her.
The Dagda is the 'good god' — good at everything, embodying abundance, wisdom, and divine fatherhood.
The Dagda's magic cauldron (never empty), club (kills/resurrects), and harp (controls seasons) make him the supreme Druidic figure.
Lugh gained entry to the divine assembly by claiming mastery of all skills simultaneously — the master of everything.
Lugh led the Tuatha Dé Danann against the Fomorians at the Second Battle of Mag Tuired.
The Morrígan appears as a crow on the battlefield, prophesies death, and tests heroes — fate and war as one.
The Morrígan embodies the sovereignty of the land — the king must mate with her (as sovereignty) to legitimately rule.
Cernunnos sits at the meeting point of human and animal worlds — the lord of wild creatures.
Cernunnos inhabits the liminal space between the ordered world and the wild — between life and death.
Osiris is murdered by Set, dismembered into 14 pieces, reassembled by Isis, and resurrected — the archetypal dying-and-rising-god.

facets.facet_name: King and Judge of the Dead
facets.core_claim: Osiris became lord of the Duat (underworld) and judge of the dead — the resurrection transformed him into the principle of afterlife justice.
Isis recovered Osiris's scattered body parts and revivified him by magic — the feminine principle of recovery and resurrection.
In the Hellenistic-Roman period Isis becomes a universal savior-goddess — direct iconographic influence on Mary-Queen-of-Heaven.
Thoth is god of writing, wisdom, magic, and the moon — divine reckoner and mediator in cosmic disputes.
Ma'at personifies cosmic order — truth, justice, right-ordering. The heart is weighed against her feather.
Thor is the Germanic/Norse thunderer — direct PIE *Perkʷunos lineage, wielder of Mjölnir.

facets.facet_name: Queen of the Realm of the Dead
facets.core_claim: Hel rules the realm of those who die of illness and old age — cold, half-dead.
Jörmungandr encircles Midgard — the Norse cosmic-serpent adversary of the thunder-god.
Allah is utterly transcendent — no image, no partner, no offspring, no likeness. The shahada: La ilaha illa Allah.
Allah possesses 99 names: al-Rahman (the Compassionate), al-Rahim (the Merciful), al-Jabbar (the Compeller), al-Qahhaar (the Subduer), al-Hayy (the Living), al-Qayyum (the Self-Subsisting)...
facets.scholarly_note: Each of the 99 names is a distinct theological statement. The tradition of recitation (dhikr) of the names is a Sufi practice.

facets.core_claim: Allah creates from nothing by command: "Be, and it is" (Kun fa-yakun, Quran 2:117). The Islamic doctrine of creatio ex nihilo is explicit.

facets.facet_name: The One Who Refused to Bow
facets.core_claim: Iblis refused to prostrate before Adam, claiming superiority by nature (fire over clay). This act of kibr (pride) is the primordial sin in Islamic theology.
Iblis was given permission to tempt humanity until the Day of Judgment. He whispers (waswasa) into human hearts, leading them toward disbelief and sin.

facets.facet_name: Bound Until the Day of Judgment
facets.core_claim: Unlike Christian Satan who is active until chained for 1000 years, Iblis operates freely until the Last Day when he will be destroyed.
The Mahdi will appear at the end of times to restore justice, pray behind Jesus (who also returns), and inaugurate the final era before the Day of Judgment.
In Twelver Shia Islam, Muhammad al-Mahdi (born 869 CE) entered occultation in 874 CE and is believed to be alive and present but hidden, communicating through intermediaries until his return.
facets.scholarly_note: The Hidden Imam doctrine is unique in world religion — a historical person believed to be alive and hidden in the world since the 9th century CE, with specific theological implications about ongoing divine guidance.

facets.core_claim: Khidr wanders the earth as an immortal guide, appearing to prophets and mystics at moments of divine initiation. His knowledge is directly from God, beyond normal prophetic transmission.

facets.facet_name: Teacher of Divine Wisdom Beyond the Law
facets.core_claim: Khidr's three acts (sinking a boat, killing a boy, building a wall without payment) violate apparent moral law but are revealed to serve higher divine purposes — the archetype of paradoxical divine wisdom.
facets.scholarly_note: The Khidr story is the Quranic locus for the tension between exoteric law (sharia) and esoteric wisdom (haqiqa) — central to Sufi thought.

facets.facet_name: The Cosmic Mirror of All Divine Attributes
facets.core_claim: The Perfect Man reflects all 99 divine names in one being — the purpose for which the cosmos was created. "I was a hidden treasure and I loved to be known, so I created the creation." (Hadith Qudsi)
In Sufi cosmology, the Qutb (pole/axis) is the living Perfect Man who holds the cosmos in balance. There is always one such person alive in the world.
facets.scholarly_note: Ibn Arabi's Wahdat al-Wujud (Unity of Being) — all existence is manifestation of the One Reality. The Perfect Man is the synthesis point. Massignon, Corbin, and Chittick are key scholars.

facets.facet_name: Word of God and Spirit (Not Divine)
facets.core_claim: Jesus is "Kalimat Allah" (Word of God) and "Ruh minhu" (Spirit from Him) — the highest titles given to any human in the Quran, but explicitly not implying divinity.
facets.scholarly_note: Islamic theology carefully distinguishes: Jesus receives divine attributes without being divine. The Quran (5:116) explicitly denies the Trinity.

facets.facet_name: The Returning Prophet Before the Last Day
facets.core_claim: Jesus will descend before the Day of Judgment, pray behind the Mahdi, break the cross (ending Christianity), kill Dajjal (the Islamic Antichrist), and die naturally.
Dajjal performs miracles (giving life to the dead, controlling rain) to deceive humanity into worshipping him. He represents the ultimate test of faith before the eschaton.

facets.facet_name: The Divine Breath That Creates the Cosmos
facets.core_claim: Creation is God's breath — the universe is the unfolding of divine self-disclosure (tajalli). The cosmos exists because God desired to be known.
facets.scholarly_note: Rumi's Masnavi opens with the reed flute crying for its origin — an allegory for the soul's longing to return to the divine source. Chittick's "The Sufi Path of Knowledge" is the key secondary source.

facets.core_claim: Mary as Queen of Heaven — crowned and enthroned, reigning with Christ, interceding for humanity. The Revelation 12 "woman clothed with the sun" is widely identified with her.
facets.scholarly_note: Hislop's identification of Mary with Semiramis/Isis is polemical Protestant apologetics. The more careful scholarship (Warner, Alone of All Her Sex; Pelikan, Mary Through the Centuries) traces legitimate iconographic continuity from Isis-with-Horus to Madonna-with-Child.

facets.core_claim: Mary mediates all graces between Christ and humanity — a theological position held by many Catholics and debated by others. She pleads the cause of sinners before divine justice.

The Virgin Mary absorbed attributes of preceding Mediterranean goddess figures: Isis (mother-with-child), Athena (wisdom/victory), Artemis/Diana (moon, virginity), Aphrodite (beauty, star of the sea).
facets.scholarly_note: Marina Warner, Alone of All Her Sex (1976) is the standard scholarly treatment of this continuity. It is descriptive, not debunking.

facets.facet_name: The Lesser YHWH — Enthroned Beside God
facets.core_claim: 3 Enoch 12: Metatron sits on a throne like God's throne at the entrance to the seventh palace. God calls him by His own name: "My name is in him" (Exodus 23:21).
facets.scholarly_note: Gershom Scholem, Major Trends in Jewish Mysticism. The "two powers in heaven" controversy — Metatron's quasi-divine status was theologically contested in Talmudic Judaism.

facets.facet_name: Heavenly Scribe of All Human Deeds
facets.core_claim: Metatron records the deeds of all humanity and Israel, and presents them before God. He is the heavenly chancellor, managing the divine bureaucracy.
In Orthodox theology, Sophia is the divine wisdom through which God creates — the blueprint of the cosmos, the "craftsman beside God" of Proverbs 8.

facets.facet_name: The World-Soul Bridging Creator and Creation
facets.core_claim: In Russian Sophiology (Solovyov/Bulgakov), Sophia is the world-soul — the eternal divine humanity, the feminine face of God, and the cosmic principle of love.
facets.scholarly_note: Bulgakov's Sophiology was condemned as heretical by the Moscow Patriarchate in 1935 but remains influential in Orthodox mystical theology. Paul Valliere, Modern Russian Theology.

facets.core_claim: God the Father has a tangible body of flesh and bones. He was once as humans are, and humans may become as He is — a complete rejection of classical theism's immaterial God.
facets.scholarly_note: The King Follett Discourse (1844) is Joseph Smith's most radical theological statement. Terryl Givens, Wrestling the Angel. LDS theology is the most significant departure from classical theism in modern Western religion.

facets.facet_name: Father of All Spirits in Pre-Mortal Life
facets.core_claim: All humans existed as spirit children of Heavenly Father and Heavenly Mother before birth into mortality — a pre-mortal existence with memory suppressed at birth.
facets.scholarly_note: The doctrine of pre-mortal existence is unique among major Christian denominations. It parallels Plato's pre-existence of souls and some Gnostic frameworks.

facets.core_claim: Heavenly Mother is deliberately hidden — God keeps her name sacred to protect her from blasphemy. Structurally: a divine feminine kept secret by the masculine divine, with significant implications.
facets.scholarly_note: Paulsen and Pulido, "A Mother There: A Survey of Historical Teachings About Mother in Heaven" (BYU Studies 2011). The doctrine is affirmed but not developed — a significant theological silence.

facets.core_claim: The Gottheit is the desert beyond all attribute — before God was God, before creation, before the Trinity. Pure silence, pure being beyond being.
facets.scholarly_note: Eckhart was condemned in 1329 but rehabilitated in scholarship. Reiner Schürmann, Meister Eckhart: Mystic and Philosopher. His Gottheit/Gott distinction parallels Kabbalah's Ein Sof/Sefirot.

facets.facet_name: The Soul's Identity with the Godhead
facets.core_claim: The Seelenfünklein (spark of the soul) is identical with the Godhead — not just similar but the same. "The eye through which I see God is the same eye through which God sees me."
Mahavira made the ford across the ocean of worldly existence — showing the path to liberation by his own crossing. He does not answer prayers; he is a model, not an intercessor.

facets.facet_name: The Tradition with No Creator God
facets.core_claim: Jainism explicitly rejects a creator god — the universe is beginningless, endless, and self-regulating by karmic law. Liberated souls (Siddhas) exist at the cosmic apex but do not create or sustain the world.
facets.scholarly_note: Jainism's explicit rejection of a creator is the most philosophically rigorous non-theism in the Indian tradition. It directly parallels Theravada Buddhism on this point. Dundas, The Jains (2002).

facets.facet_name: Liberated Souls at the Cosmic Apex
facets.core_claim: The Siddhas occupy Siddhashila at the very top of the Jain cosmos — infinite, omniscient, without body or interaction. The divine realm in Jainism is populated by formerly human beings.
facets.scholarly_note: The Jain cosmos has 14 rajju (cosmic units) of height. At the apex, above all heavens, is the Siddhashila where liberated souls exist in pure being. This is the most "apophatic" divine realm in Indian religion.

facets.core_claim: Ik Onkar is the Mool Mantar's first word — the One Creator. Sikh theology is rigorously monotheistic, rejecting Hindu polytheism, Muslim anthropomorphism, and Buddhist atheism simultaneously.
Waheguru has no form, no image, no gender — any image or idol is a distortion. The Guru Granth Sahib (the living scripture) is the form through which Waheguru is approached.
Hukam is the divine will or order that pervades and sustains all creation. By accepting Hukam, one achieves union (the central Sikh spiritual discipline). "Everything happens by Hukam."

facets.facet_name: The Living Scripture as Divine Presence
facets.core_claim: The Guru Granth Sahib is the living Guru — not a symbol of God but the actual presence of the divine Word. The scripture governs, guides, and reveals; it is consulted like a living oracle.
facets.scholarly_note: The concept of scripture as living divine presence has few parallels — the Torah scroll comes close in Judaism, but the Guru Granth's Guruship (authority to guide and govern) is unique. Nikky-Guninder Kaur Singh, The Name of My Beloved.

facets.facet_name: Sky Father, Head of the Anunnaki
facets.core_claim: Anu is the sky-father and nominal head of the Mesopotamian divine council — increasingly ceremonial, with real power delegated to Enlil and later Marduk.
Anu represents the sky as absolute — high, remote, and increasingly uninvolved. His withdrawal from active governance is an early example of the deus otiosus pattern.

facets.facet_name: Storm-King and Sovereign of the Assembly
facets.core_claim: Enlil holds the Tablets of Destiny — whoever holds them rules the cosmos. He is the active sovereign, calling divine assemblies and issuing cosmic decrees.
Enlil sends the Flood to destroy humanity because their noise disturbs his sleep — a proto-Demiurge moment where the highest active god becomes the antagonist of humanity.
facets.scholarly_note: The Atrahasis Epic (c. 1700 BCE) is the fullest version. Enlil's motivation (noise) makes him sympathetic to a point, then horrifying — the most honest divine motivation for the Flood in any tradition.

facets.facet_name: God of Wisdom, Water, and Magic
facets.core_claim: Enki possesses all the me — divine ordinances of civilization. He is the Mesopotamian god of wisdom, water, magic, and craft.

facets.facet_name: Friend of Humanity Against the Divine Council
facets.core_claim: Enki repeatedly subverts the divine council's decrees against humanity — warning of the Flood, creating humanity with Ninhursag, defending them in cosmic disputes.
Enki and Ninhursag fashion the first humans from clay and divine blood — the Mesopotamian creation-of-humanity myth.

facets.facet_name: Great Mother and Lady of the Gods
facets.core_claim: Ninhursag is the mother of the gods and co-creator of humanity — one of the four primordial Sumerian deities alongside An, Enlil, and Enki.
Ereshkigal rules the irreversible underworld — all who enter do not return without divine intervention. Her power is absolute in her realm.

facets.facet_name: God of Plague, War, and the Underworld
facets.core_claim: Nergal is the destructive face of the sun — the killing heat of summer, war, and pestilence.

facets.facet_name: Sun God of Law and Divine Justice
facets.core_claim: Shamash is the sun as the principle of justice — his light reveals all hidden deeds. He gives law to kings and judges the dead.

facets.facet_name: Primordial Salt-Water Mother of the Gods
facets.core_claim: Tiamat is the primordial salt water — from her mingling with Apsu (fresh water) the first gods are born. She is both source and adversary.

facets.facet_name: Chaos Dragon Slain to Make the Cosmos
facets.core_claim: Marduk slays Tiamat and uses her body to make the cosmos: her skull = sky, eyes = Tigris and Euphrates, ribs = vault of heaven.
Apsu is the primordial fresh water — the male counterpart of Tiamat (salt water). Their mingling is the first cosmogonic act.
Gilgamesh seeks immortality after Enkidu's death — the first full expression of humanity's refusal to accept death. He ultimately fails and returns home to his city.
Gilgamesh is 2/3 divine — the paradigmatic semi-divine hero king who builds Uruk's walls and establishes civilization.
Ra creates each day by sailing across the sky — his very presence is the light and warmth of life. He creates by speaking divine words (Heka).

facets.facet_name: Nightly Death and Rebirth in the Duat
facets.core_claim: Each night Ra dies as the setting sun, travels through the 12 hours of the Duat, and is reborn at dawn. The Amduat describes this journey in full.
Ptah creates by conceiving in his heart (thought) and speaking with his tongue (word) — the earliest documented logos theology, 2000+ years before the Gospel of John.
facets.scholarly_note: John A. Wilson's translation of the Memphite Theology in Ancient Near Eastern Texts (Pritchard). The parallels with John 1:1 are structural, not necessarily genealogical — though Egypt's influence on early Christianity is documented.

facets.facet_name: Divine Patron of Builders and Artists
facets.core_claim: Ptah is the divine craftsman — the cosmic architect whose creative act is the model for all human making.

facets.facet_name: Living Divine King — The Falcon Sun
facets.core_claim: Every living Pharaoh is Horus — the falcon who rules the sky and the earth. The Pharaoh is the point of contact between divine and human order.
Horus fights Set in the Contendings — an 80-year divine trial — to reclaim the throne stolen from his father Osiris. He is the son who restores cosmic order.
Sekhmet is the Eye of Ra — the divine wrath unleashed upon disobedient humanity. The same force that destroys is the force that heals when properly channeled.

facets.facet_name: Goddess of Plague and Its Cure
facets.core_claim: Sekhmet both sends and cures plague — her priests were the physicians of ancient Egypt. The destroyer and healer are the same force.

facets.facet_name: Goddess of Love, Beauty, and Joy
facets.core_claim: Hathor is the joy of the gods and humanity — the divine embodiment of love, music, and beauty. She welcomes the dead at the western horizon.

facets.facet_name: The Cosmic Cow — Milk of the Stars
facets.core_claim: Hathor as the cosmic cow whose milk is the Milky Way — she nurses the Pharaoh with divine milk, making him divine.

facets.facet_name: Divine Wisdom, Magic, and the Moon
facets.core_claim: Thoth possesses divine wisdom (Sia) and magical power (Heka) — the two foundational cosmic principles. He is the cosmic intelligence that orders reality.

facets.facet_name: Arbiter in Divine Disputes and Scribe of Deeds
facets.core_claim: Thoth arbitrates in the Contendings of Horus and Set, and records the verdict in the Hall of Ma'at — the divine witness and recorder.

facets.facet_name: The Body that IS the Sky
facets.core_claim: Nut's body is the sky itself — her fingers touch the western horizon, her toes the eastern. She is not in the sky; she IS the sky.

facets.facet_name: Mother Who Swallows and Births the Sun
facets.core_claim: Nut swallows Ra at sunset and gives birth to him at dawn — her body is the mechanism of the solar cycle, of death and rebirth.

facets.facet_name: The Earth God — His Body IS the Earth
facets.core_claim: Geb is the earth — not a deity who rules it but one whose body IS the ground beneath all things. Earthquakes are his laughter.

facets.facet_name: Murderer of Osiris, Usurper of the Throne
facets.core_claim: Set murders and dismembers Osiris — the original act of cosmic disruption in Egyptian theology. Yet his defeat of Apophis each night makes him necessary.

facets.facet_name: The Necessary Defender of the Solar Barque
facets.core_claim: Set stands at the prow of Ra's barque and fights Apophis each night — only his chaos-strength can overcome the chaos-serpent. Evil against evil, order maintained.

facets.facet_name: Supreme Creator and Lord of Heavens
facets.core_claim: Itzamna is the supreme Mayan deity — lord of the celestial realm, creator, and the source of civilization through his gift of writing.
Itzamna invented writing, books, and the calendar — the Mayan Thoth, the divine patron of all intellectual and scribal arts.

facets.facet_name: Descent to Xibalba — Defeating the Lords of Death
facets.core_claim: The Hero Twins descend to Xibalba and defeat its Lords not by strength but by cunning — passing every test, even death, through wit.

facets.facet_name: Ascend to Become Sun and Moon
facets.core_claim: After defeating Xibalba, the Hero Twins ascend to the sky as the Sun and Moon — creators of the current cosmic order.

facets.facet_name: The Dying and Rising Corn God
facets.core_claim: Hun Hunahpu is killed and decapitated, his body buried, his sons avenge him and resurrect him — the Mayan dying-and-rising-god tied to the corn cycle.

facets.facet_name: Rain-Storm God of the Four Directions
facets.core_claim: Chaac brings the rains that sustain life — one of the most essential and widely worshipped Mayan deities.

facets.facet_name: Moon Goddess of Medicine and Weaving
facets.core_claim: Ix Chel governs the moon, healing, weaving, and childbirth — the feminine counterpart to Itzamna.

facets.facet_name: The Twelve Lords of the Mayan Underworld
facets.core_claim: The Lords of Xibalba govern the underworld realm, inflicting specific torments on the dead. They are the Mayan parallel of the Egyptian 42 Assessors and the Gnostic Archons.

facets.facet_name: She Who Changes — The Four Seasons
facets.core_claim: Changing Woman IS the seasons — she grows old in winter and young again in spring. She embodies change as the fundamental cosmic principle.
Changing Woman mothers Monster Slayer and Born-for-Water who cleanse the world of monsters — the Navajo culture-hero pair.

facets.facet_name: Bringer of Sacred Knowledge and Ceremony
facets.core_claim: White Buffalo Calf Woman gives the Sacred Pipe and seven sacred rites — the foundational gifts that make Lakota spiritual life possible.
White Buffalo Calf Woman is expected to return — her return signals the restoration of the world. The birth of a white buffalo calf is her sign.

facets.facet_name: The Great Mystery — Sacred Power in All Things
facets.core_claim: Wakan Tanka is not a person but the sacred quality that pervades all reality — great mystery, not great man.

facets.facet_name: The Theft of the Sun — Light Bringer
facets.core_claim: Raven transforms into a pine needle, is swallowed, reborn as a baby, then steals the box containing the sun and releases light into the world.
Raven remakes the world through transformations — motivated by hunger and curiosity as much as benevolence.

facets.facet_name: The Fallen Woman Who Creates the World
facets.core_claim: Sky Woman falls from heaven and the world is made on Turtle's back beneath her — the Earth-Diver creation pattern.

facets.facet_name: Mother of the Good and Evil Twins
facets.core_claim: Sky Woman gives birth to the Good Twin (who creates beneficent things) and the Evil Twin (who creates thorns, disease, and winter) — a complete cosmic dualism born from one mother.

facets.facet_name: Her Severed Fingers Become Sea Creatures
facets.core_claim: Sedna's dismemberment creates the sea's abundance — her suffering is the origin of all marine life. The most violent creation-from-body myth.

facets.facet_name: Queen of the Undersea and Marine Life
facets.core_claim: Sedna controls whether marine animals are available to hunters — her mood determines survival. Shamans must journey to her undersea realm to comb her matted hair.

facets.facet_name: The Great Hare — Trickster and Culture Hero
facets.core_claim: Nanabozho is the quintessential Woodlands trickster-hero — stealing fire, teaching survival, and recreating the world from a grain of sand after the flood.

facets.facet_name: Sky Creator Who Satisfies All Needs
facets.core_claim: Nyame is the withdrawn sky creator — present in rain, sustaining all life, but approached through intermediaries.

facets.facet_name: The Spider Who Wins by Intelligence
facets.core_claim: Anansi defeats opponents far stronger through wit — he embodies the power of intelligence over brute force.

facets.facet_name: Owner and Keeper of All Stories
facets.core_claim: Anansi purchased all stories from the Sky God — he is the cosmic keeper of narrative memory.

facets.facet_name: Supreme Creator of the Dogon Cosmos
facets.core_claim: Amma created the universe from an egg containing the seeds (po) of all life — a fractal cosmology where the smallest contains the largest.

facets.facet_name: The Cosmic Egg Containing All Potential
facets.core_claim: Amma's egg contains all the seeds of creation — the Dogon cosmological framework is one of the most mathematically sophisticated in oral tradition.
facets.scholarly_note: Marcel Griaule's fieldwork is controversial — Walter van Beek's 1991 restudy questioned the reliability of Ogotemmeli's account. The cosmology remains remarkable regardless of how it was transmitted.

facets.facet_name: The Sacrificed and Resurrected Divine Twin
facets.core_claim: The Nommo's sacrifice and resurrection purifies the earth from Ogo's disorder — a dying-and-rising pattern in a tradition where it was independently developed.
Mawu-Lisa is the supreme dual creator — feminine/lunar/wisdom and masculine/solar/strength as inseparable aspects of one divine source.

facets.facet_name: The First Man Who Is Also Creator
facets.core_claim: Unkulunkulu emerged from reeds and created humanity — first ancestor and creator are the same person.

facets.facet_name: The Mantis Creator — Making from Thought
facets.core_claim: Cagn creates through his own substance and through thought — making the moon from a feather, creating the eland from a shoe.

facets.facet_name: The Trickster Who Dies and Returns
facets.core_claim: Cagn is sometimes killed and returns — one of the oldest documented dying-and-returning figures, in a tradition whose spiritual practices trace back 70,000+ years.
facets.scholarly_note: San rock art at sites like Drakensberg and Tsodilo Hills (UNESCO World Heritage) depicts spiritual journeys connected to Cagn's world. The San tradition is among the oldest continuously practiced spiritual traditions on earth.

facets.core_claim: Dangun combines all three cosmic realms: Hwanin (heaven), Hwanung (sky-earth mediator), and the bear-woman (earth) — their son founds civilization.

facets.facet_name: The Heavenly Son Who Descends to Earth
facets.core_claim: Hwanung descends from heaven to govern earth — the mediating divine son between transcendent heaven (Hwanin) and humanity.

facets.facet_name: The Eternal Blue Sky — Immanent and Transcendent
facets.core_claim: Tengri is not in the sky — Tengri IS the sky. The eternal blue sky is not a metaphor but the actual divine presence.

facets.facet_name: Lord of the Underworld — Adversary of the Sky
facets.core_claim: Erlik rules the underworld as the adversary of Tengri — the clearest sky/underworld dyad in Eurasian shamanism, directly paralleling Perun/Veles and Ahura Mazda/Angra Mainyu.

facets.facet_name: The Baltic Sky Father — Purest PIE Survival
facets.core_claim: Dievas is the most linguistically and culturally conservative preservation of the PIE sky-father — he retains the farmer-king character lost elsewhere.
facets.scholarly_note: Lithuanian is the most archaic living IE language. Dievas is the closest we can get to the PIE sky-father without reconstruction. Marija Gimbutas' Baltic studies and Algirdas Greimas' Of Gods and Men are key sources.

facets.facet_name: Thunder God — The PIE Thunderer Preserved
facets.core_claim: Perkunas is the most directly attested PIE thunderer — linguistically traceable to *Perkwunos.

facets.facet_name: Eternal Battle with the Cosmic Serpent
facets.core_claim: Perkunas chases Velnias the serpent — the Baltic version of the PIE *Perkwunos vs. *Ngwhi serpent battle is the most linguistically pure attestation of the formula.
Laima decrees the fate of each human at birth — an independent fate-at-birth goddess tradition.
Saule is a feminine sun goddess — a rare inversion of the typical masculine-sun pattern found across IE and non-IE traditions. She weaves, mourns, and sustains.
facets.scholarly_note: The feminine sun is structurally rare but attested in Baltic, Japanese (Amaterasu), and some Hittite-Hattic traditions (Estan). Marija Gimbutas argues this represents an Old European substrate predating IE conquest.

facets.facet_name: The Primordial Shaman Who Sings Reality
facets.core_claim: Vainamoinen creates and transforms reality through song — the singing shaman as primary creative force.

facets.facet_name: Creator of the World Through Song
facets.core_claim: In the Kalevala's creation, Vainamoinen's song is the creative force — the primordial word that brings the world into being.

facets.facet_name: The Mistress of the Cold North
facets.core_claim: Louhi hoards the Sampo (cosmic mill of abundance), steals the sun and moon, and opposes the heroes — the Finnish version of the adversary figure.
Ukko is the Finnish thunder god — part of the broad Uralic-IE thunderer tradition.
Mari is the supreme Basque deity — a mountain goddess whose tradition predates Indo-European arrival in Iberia.
facets.scholarly_note: Mari represents a possible survival of pre-IE Old European goddess religion in the Basque linguistic isolate. J.M. de Barandiaran is the foundational ethnographer.

facets.facet_name: Controller of Weather, Rewarder of Honesty
facets.core_claim: Mari punishes liars and cheats, rewards honest farmers — she is both the storm and the cosmic moral principle.

facets.facet_name: The Cosmic Serpent Consort of the Mountain
facets.core_claim: Sugaar is the serpentine consort of Mari — their periodic union generates storms. A pre-IE sky/earth + masculine-serpent/feminine-mountain divine couple.

facets.facet_name: Storm King of the Hurrian-Hittite Pantheon
facets.core_claim: Teshub is the supreme storm god of the Hurrian-Hittite world — the link between Mesopotamian and Aegean storm-god traditions.

facets.facet_name: Slayer of Illuyanka the Cosmic Serpent
facets.core_claim: Teshub defeats Illuyanka the cosmic serpent — the Hittite link in the dragon-slayer chain.
Kumarbi bites off and swallows Anu's genitals, displacing him — the direct literary prototype of Cronus castrating Uranus in Hesiod.
facets.scholarly_note: Hesiod's Theogony (c. 700 BCE) shows unmistakable structural parallels to the Kumarbi Cycle. Walter Burkert, The Orientalizing Revolution, and Martin West, The East Face of Helicon, document the transmission.

facets.core_claim: Melqart dies and is annually awakened through sacred fire — the Phoenician instance of the dying-and-rising-god pattern.
facets.scholarly_note: The egersis (awakening) ceremony is attested in Josephus. Melqart's identification with Herakles is documented by Herodotus. Bonnet, Melqart is the standard scholarly treatment.

facets.facet_name: The Face of Baal — Carthaginian Great Goddess
facets.core_claim: Tanit is the supreme goddess of Carthage — her symbol the most widely attested Punic icon.

facets.facet_name: The Absolute Beyond Being and Thought
facets.core_claim: The One is beyond being — it does not even know itself, because knowing requires subject/object duality. It is pure simplicity, the ground of all.
facets.scholarly_note: Plotinus' Enneads are the philosophical summit of the apophatic tradition in Western thought. Pierre Hadot, Plotinus or the Simplicity of Vision is the key modern treatment.

facets.facet_name: Source of Emanation — Nous, Psyche, Matter
facets.core_claim: From the One emanates Nous (Divine Intellect, containing all Forms), then Psyche (World Soul), then matter — the full Neoplatonic cosmos.

facets.facet_name: God of Beginnings — Before All Other Gods
facets.core_claim: In Roman prayer, Janus is invoked first before all other gods — he is the beginning itself, through whom all other invocations must pass.
Janus guards all transitions — doorways, beginnings of months, years, journeys, and enterprises. He is the liminal moment made divine.
Sol Invictus was Rome's attempt at solar monotheism before Christianity triumphed — sharing imagery (radiate crown, December 25) that influenced early Christian iconography.
facets.scholarly_note: Franz Cumont's work on solar theology in late antiquity. The December 25 date is documented for Sol Invictus before its adoption for Christmas (Thomas Talley, Origins of the Liturgical Year).

facets.facet_name: Torn Apart and Reborn — The Orphic Mystery
facets.core_claim: Zagreus is the paradigmatic Orphic dying-and-rising mystery — his dismemberment and rebirth is the cosmic event that generates human souls.
Orphic theology: humans are made from Titanic ash mixed with Zagreus — we contain a divine spark (the Dionysiac) imprisoned in a Titanic body.
facets.scholarly_note: The Orphic anthropology — divine soul imprisoned in matter — directly parallels Gnostic theology. Walter Burkert, Ancient Mystery Cults, is the standard treatment.

facets.core_claim: Kali is Mahakala (Great Time) — she consumes even Shiva himself. Her destruction is liberation from attachment to form.
Kali annihilates the ego-self to reveal the eternal — destruction as the path to liberation.
Tara saves from the ocean of worldly existence through fierce compassion — the roar that destroys demons is the sound of liberation.

facets.facet_name: The Supreme Goddess of the Sri Yantra
facets.core_claim: Tripura Sundari governs the Sri Yantra — the geometric map of the cosmos and of consciousness itself. She is the totality of beauty, knowledge, and bliss.
facets.scholarly_note: The Sri Vidya tradition is among the most sophisticated geometric-cosmological systems in world religion. Douglas Renfrew Brooks, The Secret of the Three Cities.

facets.core_claim: Bhuvaneshvari's body is not in the universe — the universe is within her body.

facets.facet_name: Fierce Destroyer of Ego and Ignorance
facets.core_claim: Bhairavi destroys everything that prevents liberation — including the devotee's comfortable illusions.
Chhinnamasta cuts off her own head and feeds others with her blood while also drinking it herself — the paradox that the source and the sacrifice are the same being.
facets.scholarly_note: Chhinnamasta is perhaps the most theologically paradoxical image in world religion — the creator who destroys herself to feed, and is herself fed. David Kinsley, Tantric Visions of the Divine Feminine.

facets.facet_name: The Void — What Remains After Destruction
facets.core_claim: Dhumavati is the divine void — she represents the state before creation and after destruction. She has no consort because she preceded all duality.
Bagalamukhi holds the tongue of her enemy — she is the power of silence and stillness over all movement and speech.

facets.facet_name: Wisdom from the Margins — The Outcaste Goddess
facets.core_claim: Matangi rules speech and music from the margins — her wisdom comes from outside the pure center of society.
facets.scholarly_note: Matangi represents Tantric inversion of caste purity — the sacred found in the polluted. A theological statement about the omnipresence of the divine.

facets.core_claim: Kamala is Lakshmi in her Tantric aspect — beauty, prosperity, and abundance as divine gifts available to all.
Durga is the concentration of all divine power into the feminine warrior — she defeats what no male god could.

facets.facet_name: Shakti — The Concentrated Power of All Gods
facets.core_claim: Durga IS the shakti (divine energy) of all the gods — she does not receive their weapons but manifests as their combined power.
Ganesha removes obstacles for devotees — and places them for others. He is the principle of appropriately timed difficulty.
Ganesha is invoked before all other deities — he is the beginning of all sacred activity.

facets.facet_name: The Cosmic Dance That IS the Universe
facets.core_claim: Nataraja's dance is not a metaphor — the cosmos IS his dance. When he stops dancing, the universe ceases.
Nataraja performs five acts simultaneously: creation (drum), preservation (abhaya mudra), destruction (fire), veiling (foot on ignorance), and grace (raised foot).
facets.scholarly_note: Ananda Coomaraswamy's The Dance of Shiva (1918) introduced Nataraja to Western scholarship. The five-act theology (Panchakriya) is the pinnacle of Shaiva Siddhanta cosmology.

facets.core_claim: Vairochana transforms ignorance into all-encompassing wisdom — he is the dharmadhatu (realm of all dharmas) in its luminous aspect.
Akshobhya's mirror-like wisdom reflects all phenomena without distortion, bias, or judgment.

facets.facet_name: Equalizing Wisdom — All Beings Have Equal Worth
facets.core_claim: Ratnasambhava's equalizing wisdom sees the equal value of all beings — no hierarchies in buddha-nature.
Amitabha's discriminating wisdom distinguishes all phenomena without attachment — and his compassion creates the Pure Land for all who call his name.
Amoghasiddhi accomplishes all actions without obstruction — his wisdom manifests as fearless action in the world.

facets.facet_name: The Flaming Sword That Cuts Ignorance
facets.core_claim: Manjushri's sword cuts through all conceptual fabrication — not killing but liberating from the prison of ignorance.

facets.facet_name: Vow to Empty All Hell Realms Before Achieving Buddhahood
facets.core_claim: Ksitigarbha made the greatest bodhisattva vow — he will not rest until all hell realms are empty. His compassion exceeds even that of Avalokitesvara in its scope.

facets.facet_name: Descender to Hell Realms — The Ultimate Psychopomp
facets.core_claim: Ksitigarbha actively descends to all the hell realms to teach and liberate — the most radical descent-to-the-underworld figure in Buddhism.
Yemoja is the mother of the orishas and the divine mother of all Yoruba people — her waters sustain and her protection is fierce.

facets.facet_name: Deified King — Thunder and Royal Power
facets.core_claim: Shango is the thunder-king — the paradigm of the deified royal ancestor whose power persists after death as cosmic force.

facets.facet_name: Iron as the Ambivalent Foundation of Civilization
facets.core_claim: Ogun is the iron that builds and destroys — the ambivalent foundation of all technological civilization.

facets.facet_name: Goddess of Storms and Death Transitions
facets.core_claim: Oya governs the whirlwind, the marketplace, and the transition from life to death — she is change itself.

facets.facet_name: Orisha of Disease and Its Cure
facets.core_claim: Babalu Aye causes disease and heals it — he is both the affliction and the remedy.

facets.facet_name: The Witness of Destiny — Oracle of Ifa
facets.core_claim: Orunmila witnessed all of creation and knows every soul's destiny — the Ifa oracle is the most elaborate divination system in Africa.

facets.facet_name: Arch-Orisha Who Shapes All Human Bodies
facets.core_claim: Obatala is the highest-ranking orisha in spiritual purity — the divine sculptor who gives each human their physical form.

facets.facet_name: Queen of the Immortals — Guardian of Eternal Life
facets.core_claim: Xi Wangmu guards the peaches of immortality and rules over all female immortals — the feminine principle of eternal life in Chinese tradition.

facets.facet_name: The Deified General — Apotheosis of Virtue
facets.core_claim: Guan Yu's deification shows how historical virtue can become cosmic principle — he embodies the Confucian ideal elevated to divine status.

facets.facet_name: Goddess of Love and War — Collector of the Slain
facets.core_claim: Freya receives half the battle-slain — she is simultaneously the goddess of erotic love and of warriors' death.
Freya taught Odin the shamanic art of seidr — she is the original practitioner of Norse magic.

facets.facet_name: The Beloved God Whose Death Undoes the World
facets.core_claim: Baldur's death is the beginning of the end — all creation weeps for him. His death by treachery despite universal protection parallels the dying-god pattern.

facets.facet_name: The God Who Returns in the New World
facets.core_claim: After Ragnarok and the world's rebirth, Baldur returns from Hel to rule the new earth.

facets.facet_name: Queen Who Knows All Fates but Speaks of None
facets.core_claim: Frigg sees all fates but speaks of none — the divine feminine who holds cosmic knowledge in silence.

facets.facet_name: Lord of the Sea and Earthquakes
facets.core_claim: Poseidon rules the untameable sea and causes earthquakes — his power challenges even Zeus's authority.
Artemis governs the liminal wilderness, the moon, and the protection of young women at the threshold of adulthood.

facets.facet_name: War as Pure Violence and Terror
facets.core_claim: Ares is the divine principle of war as sheer destruction — even the other gods despise him.

facets.facet_name: The Divine Craftsman — Creating Through Skill
facets.core_claim: Hephaestus is the divine principle of skilled creation — what the mind designs, the hands can make.
Tlaloc brings the rains that sustain all agricultural civilization — he has been worshipped in Mesoamerica for over 3,000 years.

facets.facet_name: Lord of Tlalocan — Paradise of the Water Dead
facets.core_claim: Tlaloc receives those who die by water — they go to Tlalocan, a paradise of abundance and rest.

facets.facet_name: The Devouring Earth That Births and Consumes
facets.core_claim: Coatlicue is the earth as both womb and grave — she births the gods and receives the dead.

facets.facet_name: The Flayed God Whose Death Generates Renewal
facets.core_claim: Xipe Totec must be killed and flayed to generate the new agricultural season — the most visceral expression of the dying-god pattern.

facets.facet_name: Sky Father in Eternal Embrace with Earth
facets.core_claim: Ranginui and Papatuanuku are the primordial union — the universe exists in the darkness between their bodies until their children separate them.

facets.facet_name: His Tears Are the Rain That Falls on Earth
facets.core_claim: Ranginui weeps for his separation from Papatuanuku — his tears are dew and rain. Their grief sustains the world.

facets.facet_name: The Earth Mother — Her Body IS the World
facets.core_claim: Papatuanuku is not on the earth — she IS the earth. All living things are her children.

facets.facet_name: The Separator Who Makes the World Possible
facets.core_claim: Tane separates sky from earth — creating the space in which life can exist. The separation IS the creation.

facets.facet_name: Lord of Forests and All That Lives in Them
facets.core_claim: Tane clothes his mother Papatuanuku with trees and fills the sky with birds.

facets.facet_name: The Maker of New Land from the Ocean
facets.core_claim: Hawaii itself is Pele's creation — the islands formed by volcanic eruption rising from the sea floor.

facets.facet_name: The Dawn Maiden Become Queen of the Dead
facets.core_claim: Hine-nui-te-po's transformation from dawn goddess to death queen through the discovery of incest is the Maori origin of death.

facets.facet_name: Creator of Humanity from His Own Image
facets.core_claim: Kane created the first man in his own image from red earth — the Hawaiian parallel to biblical Adam.

facets.facet_name: God of War and Masculine Power
facets.core_claim: Ku is the principle of masculine power in all its forms — from war to politics to fishing.

facets.facet_name: God of Fertility and the Peace That Feeds
facets.core_claim: Lono governs the wet season and agriculture — his presence means abundance, his absence famine.

facets.facet_name: Active Sky Creator Who Taught Humanity
facets.core_claim: Baiame created the world and taught humans to survive — an active creator who remains connected through the sky.

facets.facet_name: His Tracks in the Milky Way
facets.core_claim: Baiame's tracks through the sky are visible as the Milky Way — the cosmos bears the trace of the creator's movement.
Bunjil is the divine eagle who made everything — the creator whose form is the apex predator of the sky.

facets.facet_name: Sibling Creators Who Birth the World
facets.core_claim: The Djanggawul siblings create the world by walking it and planting sacred poles — creation as embodied journey.
Altjira is perhaps the most complete deus otiosus in world religion — he created everything and then utterly ceased to interact with his creation.
facets.scholarly_note: The deus otiosus pattern (creator who withdraws) is documented across dozens of traditions. Schmidt, The Origin and Growth of Religion. Altjira is the most extreme form.

facets.core_claim: Qat makes people from wood and animates them with music — creation through performance.
Inti is the sun as political and cosmic sovereignty — the Inca Sapa Inca is his son on earth.

facets.facet_name: Divine Father of the Royal Line
facets.core_claim: Every Inca emperor is the son of Inti — divine right derives from direct solar descent.

facets.facet_name: Moon Mother — Governess of Time and Women
facets.core_claim: Mama Quilla governs the lunar calendar and women — the feminine principle of cosmic time.

facets.facet_name: The Earth That Must Be Fed in Return
facets.core_claim: Andean theology of ayni (reciprocity) requires feeding Pachamama — she gives food, you return libations. The most active reciprocal cosmology in the Americas.

facets.facet_name: Lord of the Inner Earth and Dead
facets.core_claim: Supay rules Uku Pacha — the world below ground where the dead dwell.

facets.facet_name: Creator Whose Chant Sustains the Cosmos
facets.core_claim: The cosmos is Nande Ru's ongoing chant — his word IS the world. When the song ends, the world ends.

facets.facet_name: Keeper of Rita — The Cosmic Moral Law
facets.core_claim: Varuna upholds Rita (the cosmic moral order) — the Vedic equivalent of Maat, Asha, and Dharma.
Agni carries the sacrifice upward — he is the divine postal system, the link between the human and divine realms.

facets.facet_name: The Divine Fire That Is in All Fires
facets.core_claim: Every fire IS Agni — the divine is immanent in the simplest human act of making fire.

facets.facet_name: Storm King and Wielder of the Thunderbolt
facets.core_claim: Indra is the Vedic king of heaven — his vajra strikes like lightning and his chariot is the storm.

facets.facet_name: Slayer of Vritra the Cosmic Dragon
facets.core_claim: Indra slays Vritra and releases the cosmic waters — the Vedic instance of the PIE dragon-slayer formula.

facets.facet_name: The Sacred Plant That Is Also a God
facets.core_claim: Soma is simultaneously the pressed plant, the ritual drink, and a divine being — the sacrament and the deity are identical.
facets.scholarly_note: Wasson and Ruck proposed Amanita muscaria as the Soma plant; Flattery proposed Harmaline-containing Peganum harmala (Syrian rue). The botanical identification remains debated.

facets.facet_name: The Drink of Immortality That Sustains the Gods
facets.core_claim: The gods themselves need soma to maintain their divinity — it is the substance of divine life.
Izanagi and Izanami stir the primordial ocean and create Japan — the sexual union of heaven (Izanagi) and earth (Izanami) generates the world.

facets.facet_name: Creates the Three Great Kami Through Purification
facets.core_claim: Izanagi's ritual purification after Yomi generates Amaterasu (from left eye), Tsukuyomi (right eye), and Susanoo (nose) — the three sovereign deities of Japan emerge from an act of cleansing.
Tsukuyomi rules the night sky — his estrangement from Amaterasu creates the permanent separation of day and night.

facets.facet_name: His Violence Creates the Separation of Day and Night
facets.core_claim: Amaterasu's refusal to look at Tsukuyomi after his murder of Ukemochi establishes the alternation of day and night — disorder creates cosmic structure.

facets.facet_name: Guardian of Rice, Commerce, and Worldly Success
facets.core_claim: Inari blesses rice fields and business ventures — the most practically worshipped kami, present in every aspect of daily economic life.
Susanoo's grief and violence cause his expulsion from heaven — the storm as divine grief made manifest.

facets.facet_name: The Cosmic Bureaucracy as Divine Governance
facets.core_claim: The Jade Emperor's heaven is a perfect bureaucracy — the universe governed by cosmic civil servants with specific portfolios.
facets.scholarly_note: The Chinese celestial bureaucracy is one of the most institutionally articulated cosmological systems in world religion — theology mapped onto administrative law.

facets.facet_name: The One Who Hears the World's Cries
facets.core_claim: Guanyin responds to every cry of suffering — her compassion is unconditional and universal.

facets.facet_name: Thousand Arms to Help All Beings Simultaneously
facets.core_claim: Guanyin's thousand arms each hold a different implement of aid — she can help everyone simultaneously.

facets.facet_name: The Deified Human Who Protects Sailors
facets.core_claim: Mazu is a historical person who became divine through exceptional compassion — still actively worshipped by hundreds of millions.

facets.facet_name: Six Divine Emanations of Ahura Mazda
facets.core_claim: The Amesha Spentas are the first emanations of Ahura Mazda — six aspects of divine goodness through which the world is created and sustained.
facets.scholarly_note: The Amesha Spentas influenced Jewish angelology and through it, Christian and Islamic heavenly hierarchies. Mary Boyce, Zoroastrians.

facets.facet_name: Father of Both Good and Evil
facets.core_claim: Zurvan's doubt generates evil (Angra Mainyu) and his sacrifice generates good (Ahura Mazda) — the most philosophically honest treatment of the origin of evil in Zoroastrian tradition.
facets.scholarly_note: Zurvanism was the dominant form of Zoroastrianism in the Sasanian period. Zaehner, Zurvan: A Zoroastrian Dilemma is the standard scholarly treatment.

facets.core_claim: Arawn rules Annwn — a realm that is not punishment but a parallel world of feasting and pleasure, accessible to the worthy.

facets.facet_name: Sovereignty Goddess — She Chooses the King
facets.core_claim: Rhiannon represents the land's sovereignty — she chooses her husband, legitimizing his kingship.
Rhiannon is the Welsh horse goddess — directly cognate with Gaulish Epona.

facets.facet_name: Lord of the Sea and the Otherworld
facets.core_claim: Manannan rules both the physical sea and the Otherworld realm — sea and Otherworld are the same liminal space.

facets.facet_name: Guide of Souls to the Isles of the Blessed
facets.core_claim: Manannan guides the dead to Tir na nOg — the Celtic psychopomp.

facets.facet_name: Triple Goddess of Fire — Poetry, Craft, Healing
facets.core_claim: Brigid governs the three sacred fires: the fire of inspiration (poetry), the fire of the forge (smithcraft), and the fire of the hearth (healing).

facets.facet_name: The Winter Hag Who Shapes the Land
facets.core_claim: The Cailleach shapes the landscape by dropping her boulder-load and rules winter — she is the destructive-creative face of the earth goddess.

facets.facet_name: Divine Horse Goddess — Sovereignty and Fertility
facets.core_claim: Epona is the horse as divine sovereignty — the land and its political right-to-rule expressed through the horse.
All animals gather around Cernunnos — he is the lord of the living world.
Jarilo dies each year to bring the harvest — the Slavic dying-and-rising god whose cycle IS the agricultural year.
Ulgen is the benevolent sky pole of the Siberian shamanic cosmos — his goodness requires Erlik's opposition to define it.

facets.facet_name: The Cosmic Axis Connecting All Worlds
facets.core_claim: The World Tree is the structural principle of shamanic cosmology — the axis along which the shaman travels between worlds.
facets.scholarly_note: Mircea Eliade's Shamanism: Archaic Techniques of Ecstasy documented the World Tree as a universal shamanic structure. Joseph Campbell extended this in The Hero with a Thousand Faces.

facets.facet_name: The Four-Faced Creator of Worldly Blessing
facets.core_claim: Phra Phrom's four faces see all directions simultaneously — nothing escapes his awareness or his blessing.
The Four Immortals govern the sacred domains of Vietnamese life — mountain, children, heaven, and cultivation.

facets.facet_name: The King as Living God on Earth
facets.core_claim: The Khmer Devaraja is the political and cosmological center — the king's body is the axis of the world.

facets.facet_name: The Crossroads Gate — Keeper of All Communication
facets.core_claim: Papa Legba opens the gate between worlds — no communication with the divine is possible without his permission.

facets.facet_name: The Bawdy Lord of Death and Resurrection
facets.core_claim: Baron Samedi is death as comedy — he controls whether the sick live or die, and chooses with irreverent humor.

facets.facet_name: The Benevolent and Wrathful Sky God — One Being
facets.core_claim: Engai is one being with two faces — the Black God who gives rain and cattle, the Red God who sends lightning and punishment.
The Pleroma is the fullness of divine reality — 30 Aeons emanating in pairs from the primal Bythos-Sige. The material world is a fallen fragment.
facets.scholarly_note: Elaine Pagels, The Gnostic Gospels. Valentinianism is the most philosophically sophisticated Gnostic system. Its aeon-structure directly parallels Kabbalistic sefirot.

facets.facet_name: The Ignorant Creator — Does Not Know the True God
facets.core_claim: Yaldabaoth creates the material world without knowing the higher divine reality — his creation is real but spiritually impoverished.
facets.scholarly_note: The Gnostic identification of the biblical God as an ignorant demiurge is hermeneutically based on Exodus 20:5 ("I am a jealous God") — jealousy implying ignorance of a higher deity. Pagels, The Gnostic Gospels.

facets.facet_name: His Boast Reveals He Does Not Know the True God
facets.core_claim: "I am a jealous God; there is no other God beside me" — in Gnostic reading, this boast reveals Yaldabaoth's ignorance: you are only jealous if you fear competition.

facets.facet_name: The Divine Mother — First Emanation of the Father
facets.core_claim: Barbelo is the first thought of the Invisible Spirit — she is the womb of the divine realm, the mother of the Son before all things.
Barbelo descends three times to the material world to try to redeem the trapped divine sparks — the Gnostic redeemer figure.

facets.facet_name: The Endless — Absolutely Unknowable and Undescribable
facets.core_claim: Ein Sof cannot be described, named, or thought — all attributes belong to the Sefirot, not to Ein Sof itself.

facets.facet_name: The Ein Sof Manifests Through the Ten Sefirot
facets.core_claim: The Ten Sefirot are the channels through which Ein Sof manifests — not separate from Ein Sof but its self-revelation.

facets.facet_name: Ten Divine Attributes Through Which God Creates
facets.core_claim: The Sefirot are simultaneously divine attributes, cosmic energies, and the structure of reality — the universe is built from divine self-revelation.

facets.facet_name: The Tree of Life — Map of the Divine and Human
facets.core_claim: The Tree of Life is the structural map of reality — mirroring above and below, divine and human, cosmic and personal.
facets.scholarly_note: The Zohar's Tree of Life is the most comprehensive structural map of divine reality in Jewish mysticism. Scholem, Kabbalah. The Qliphoth (shells/husks) — the demonic mirror of the Sefirot — are equally important.

facets.facet_name: The Cosmic Human Whose Body Is the Universe
facets.core_claim: Adam Kadmon is the first form taken by divine light — the cosmic template of humanity and the universe simultaneously.
Nareau the spider creates from thought — the divine mind as spider spinning the web of reality.

facets.facet_name: The Hidden Supreme God Known Only to Priests
facets.core_claim: Io is the supreme Maori deity known only to the esoteric priesthood — the inner teaching beneath the popular mythology.

facets.facet_name: God of Learning, Wind, and Civilization
facets.core_claim: Quetzalcoatl gave humanity maize, writing, the calendar, and the arts — the Mesoamerican divine civilizer.

facets.facet_name: The Returning God Whose Year Coincided with Conquest
facets.core_claim: Quetzalcoatl departed eastward and promised to return in One Reed year — 1519 CE. The timing of Spanish arrival shaped the Aztec response to Cortes.
facets.scholarly_note: Whether Moctezuma actually believed Cortes was Quetzalcoatl is debated by scholars (Townsend, The Aztecs). The myth's impact on the conquest is not debated.

facets.core_claim: Mictlantecuhtli rules the Aztec underworld — the dead must navigate nine levels and four years to reach him.
In the Arctic, the return of the sun is the return of life itself — Beaivvas is the most existentially necessary deity.

facets.facet_name: The Sami Mother Who Receives Souls from God
facets.core_claim: Madderakkha is the cosmic intermediary of birth — she receives soul-substance from the divine and channels it into each new human life.

facets.facet_name: God of Song Who Gave Voice to All Life
facets.core_claim: Vanemuine's primordial song gave all living beings their individual voices — creation through music.

facets.facet_name: The Inspired God of Wisdom and the Dead
facets.core_claim: Woden is Odin — the divine patron of Anglo-Saxon kingship, inspiration, and battle-death.

facets.facet_name: Spring-Dawn Goddess Whose Name Survived in Easter
facets.core_claim: Eostre is the dawn/spring goddess — the most linguistically significant figure in Anglo-Saxon religion because her name survived into Christianity.
facets.scholarly_note: Bede is the only source. Philip Shaw, Pagan Goddesses in the Early Germanic World argues for authenticity against Ronald Hutton's skepticism. The Easter etymology from *h2ewsos (dawn) is uncontested.

facets.facet_name: Earth Mother in the Sacred Wagon
facets.core_claim: Nerthus's wagon procession brings peace — her presence is the peace of the land.

facets.facet_name: The Three Mother Goddesses of the Rhine
facets.core_claim: The Matronae are the most archaeologically attested deity-form in pre-Christian Germany — three mothers as the fundamental sacred triad.

facets.facet_name: Etruscan Sky King with Three Thunderbolts
facets.core_claim: Tinia is the supreme Etruscan sky god — the direct prototype of Roman Jupiter.

facets.facet_name: Death Demon and Driver of Souls to the Underworld
facets.core_claim: Charun drives souls to the underworld with his hammer — not a gentle guide but a terrifying escort.
Gaia does not rule the earth — she IS the earth. The first solid reality after Chaos.

facets.facet_name: First Owner of Delphi — The Earth Speaks
facets.core_claim: Delphi was originally Gaia's oracle — the earth itself spoke prophecy before Apollo took it.

facets.facet_name: Queen of the Dead — Compassionate Ruler
facets.core_claim: Persephone becomes a compassionate queen of the dead — more approachable than Hades.

facets.facet_name: Mistress of the Crossroads and Magic
facets.core_claim: Hekate stands at the crossroads where three roads meet — the liminal space between all worlds.

facets.facet_name: Goddess of Night, the Moon, and Witchcraft
facets.core_claim: Hekate governs the night sky, the moon, and magical practice — the divine patron of all who work with hidden powers.
Hermes carries messages between gods and humans and between the living and the dead.
Hermes steals Apollo's cattle on his first day of life and invents the lyre — the divine trickster who creates culture through transgression.
Demeter is the grain that feeds all humanity — her gift is civilization itself.

facets.facet_name: Her Grief Creates Winter, Her Joy Creates Spring
facets.core_claim: Demeter's emotional state IS the seasons — divine grief as cosmological principle.

facets.facet_name: King of the Gods and Keeper of Law
facets.core_claim: Jupiter is the supreme Roman deity — the sky-law principle that underlies both cosmic and human legal order.
Mars is uniquely Roman — agricultural protection and warfare are the same divine function, the defense of the community.

facets.facet_name: Love Goddess and Divine Ancestor of Rome
facets.core_claim: Venus is love as political power — her divine descent legitimizes Roman imperial claims.

facets.facet_name: Huntress, Moon, and the Sacred Grove
facets.core_claim: Diana is Artemis in her Italian form — but the Arician grove ritual gives her a uniquely chthonic dimension.

facets.facet_name: The Eternal Fire That Is the City's Life
facets.core_claim: Vesta's flame is not symbolic of Rome — it IS Rome's life force. If it dies, Rome dies.

facets.facet_name: Lord of Prophecy and the Oracle at Delphi
facets.core_claim: Apollo speaks the future through the Pythia at Delphi — the divine word that shapes human destiny.

facets.facet_name: The Sun of Arts, Music, and Rational Order
facets.core_claim: Apollo is the sun as rational beauty — the principle that order, harmony, and art are the same thing.

facets.facet_name: Love and Desire as Cosmic Forces
facets.core_claim: Aphrodite is love as an irresistible cosmic force — not sentiment but the primal drive that moves the world.

facets.facet_name: Wisdom — Born from the Divine Mind
facets.core_claim: Athena is born from Zeus's head — she IS divine thought made manifest.

facets.facet_name: Strategic War and the Defense of Civilization
facets.core_claim: Athena is war as intelligence and strategy — the opposite of Ares's brutal force.

facets.facet_name: Upholder of Oaths, Guests, and Suppliants
facets.core_claim: Zeus Xenios (of guests) and Zeus Horkios (of oaths) — the sky god as the enforcer of human moral bonds.
Krishna's flute draws all souls as the gopis are drawn — divine love as the cosmological principle.

facets.facet_name: The Cosmic Form — I Am Time Itself
facets.core_claim: "I am become Death, the destroyer of worlds" (Gita 11:32) — Krishna reveals himself as the totality of cosmic time.

facets.facet_name: Destruction as the Gateway to Liberation
facets.core_claim: Shiva's destruction is not evil but necessary — he destroys what is worn out to make space for renewal.

facets.facet_name: The Supreme Yogi — Lord of Meditation
facets.core_claim: Shiva is the ideal of the renunciant — his stillness is the transcendence of all desire and the ground of liberation.

facets.facet_name: Creator and Sovereign of the Pure Land
facets.core_claim: Amitabha created the Pure Land through merit accumulated over eons — a paradise realm where liberation is guaranteed.

facets.facet_name: Salvation by Faith Alone — The 18th Vow
facets.core_claim: Amitabha's 18th vow: all who sincerely call his name even 10 times will be reborn in the Pure Land. The Buddhist parallel to grace-based salvation.
facets.scholarly_note: The Pure Land tradition is the largest Buddhist school worldwide. The tension between tariki (other-power/grace) and jiriki (self-power/effort) mirrors the Protestant/Catholic grace debate. Shinran, Tannisho.

facets.facet_name: Lord of White — Purity and Moral Clarity
facets.core_claim: Obatala governs purity and clarity — white cloth, white light, and the moral clarity that allows human dignity.

facets.facet_name: The Divine Child Who Dismembers Himself for His Parents
facets.core_claim: Nezha cuts off his own flesh and bones to spare his parents from divine punishment — the ultimate act of filial sacrifice.

facets.facet_name: Reborn from Lotus Flowers — The Divine Child
facets.core_claim: Nezha is reborn from lotus flowers planted by his teacher — death becomes the gateway to a purer, more powerful existence.

facets.facet_name: Supreme Earth Mother and Water Goddess
facets.core_claim: Atabey is the Taino supreme goddess — earth and water as the divine feminine principle.

facets.facet_name: Lord of the Life-Giving Cassava and Sea
facets.core_claim: Yucahu provides both the staple crop (cassava) and the sea's bounty — the masculine principle of sustenance.
Tezcatlipoca sees all through his obsidian mirror — every human deed, every hidden thought.

facets.facet_name: The Rival Who Ends Cosmic Ages
facets.core_claim: Tezcatlipoca creates and destroys the five cosmic ages — he and Quetzalcoatl are the paired forces of cosmic creation and destruction.
The were-jaguar represents the shamanic capacity to transform — the boundary between human and animal is permeable to the sacred.
Tabiti is invoked in the most sacred Scythian oaths — fire as the supreme sacred principle among the steppe peoples.

facets.facet_name: The Ecstatic Great Mother of the Gods
facets.core_claim: Cybele is the mountain mother — her ecstatic rites (drums, castration, wild dancing) access divine reality through the dissolution of ego.

facets.facet_name: The Castrated God Who Rises at Spring
facets.core_claim: Attis dies and rises — his resurrection on the Day of Joy (March 25) is the spring festival.

facets.facet_name: The Tauroctony — Slaying the Cosmic Bull
facets.core_claim: Mithras slays the cosmic bull and from its death springs all life — the most enigmatic mystery-religion image of antiquity.
facets.scholarly_note: Cumont's solar theology interpretation of Mithras is largely superseded. Beck, The Religion of the Mithras Cult in the Roman Empire argues for an astral theology. The tauroctony remains undeciphered.

facets.facet_name: The Hidden God Made Manifest as the Sun
facets.core_claim: Amun (the hidden) merged with Ra (the visible) creates the supreme Egyptian paradox: the transcendent made immanent.
facets.scholarly_note: The merger of Amun and Ra is the Egyptian resolution of the apophatic-cataphatic tension: the hidden god IS the sun. Jan Assmann, Egyptian Solar Theology.

facets.core_claim: Serapis synthesizes Osiris, Apis, and Greek Zeus/Pluto — the most deliberately constructed syncretic deity in the ancient world.

facets.facet_name: The Grandmother Who Thought the World
facets.core_claim: Spider Grandmother thought existence into being — creation as an act of divine thinking.

facets.facet_name: The Eternal Sky God of the Oromo
facets.core_claim: Waaqa is the eternal, benevolent sky god — his ayyaana (spirit) pervades all living things.

facets.facet_name: The Bear as Divine Guest — Sacred Reciprocity
facets.core_claim: The Ainu bear ceremony (Iyomante) returns the bear's spirit (kamuy) to the divine world — the most fully developed sacred-animal ritual in northeast Asia.

facets.facet_name: The Supreme Bon Teacher — Pre-Buddhist Tibet
facets.core_claim: Gshen-rab is the teacher who revealed Bon — the indigenous Tibetan spiritual tradition that predates and was transformed by Buddhist contact.

facets.facet_name: Her Vow to Kill 1000 Per Day Creates Mortality
facets.core_claim: Izanami vows to kill 1000 people per day; Izanagi counters he will create 1500 births — mortality and birth are established in one exchange.

facets.facet_name: The Cave of Heaven — Light Returns Through Laughter
facets.core_claim: When Amaterasu hides in the cave, the world darkens. The other gods' laughter and dance draws her out — joy restores light.

facets.facet_name: Emperor of War, Righteousness, and Commerce
facets.core_claim: Kuan Ti's apotheosis as emperor-god makes historical virtue cosmic law.

facets.facet_name: Divine Couple Who Create Through Union
facets.core_claim: Izanagi and Izanami are the first divine couple — their sexual union creates the world.
Unetlanvhi pervades all things while being beyond any single form — the Cherokee parallel of the Great Mystery.
Selu instructs her sons to kill her — her body becomes the corn that feeds the people. Sacrifice as the origin of agricultural abundance.

facets.facet_name: The Original Hunter — Lord of Game
facets.core_claim: Kanati knew all animals before they were freed — he is the divine principle of the hunt and the relationship with game.
Usen is not actively involved in the world but is the underlying source from which all life draws breath.

facets.facet_name: Mountain Spirits Who Taught the Apache
facets.core_claim: The Gaan are the supernatural teachers of Apache culture — they dance among the people in the Crown Dance ceremony.

facets.facet_name: Slayer of Monsters — Cleansing the World
facets.core_claim: Monster Slayer clears the world of supernatural threats — the Navajo hero who makes ordinary life possible.

facets.facet_name: The Cunning Twin — Wisdom Beside Violence
facets.core_claim: Born-for-Water complements his brother's violence with wisdom and spiritual practice — the necessary dual aspect of the hero.

facets.facet_name: The Hopi Supreme Creator — Before All
facets.core_claim: Taiowa exists alone before all creation — the Hopi monad from whom all emanates.

facets.facet_name: The Cosmic Architect Who Shaped the Universe
facets.core_claim: Sotuknang is the first emanation from Taiowa — the divine craftsman who executes the creator's plan.

facets.facet_name: Guardian of the Fourth World and the Dead
facets.core_claim: Masauu greets the dead at death and greets the living at emergence — he is the guardian of the boundary between worlds.

facets.facet_name: Ancestral Spirits Who Visit Half the Year
facets.core_claim: The kachinas are not gods but ancestral and nature-spirits — they descend from the mountains to bring blessing during winter and spring.

facets.facet_name: The Spider Trickster Who Creates and Deceives
facets.core_claim: Inktomi creates the world and then becomes its most reliable deceiver — culture is born from his cunning.

facets.facet_name: Storm Beings Whose Wings Are Thunder
facets.core_claim: The Thunderbirds bring rain and protect humans from chaos — too sacred to be looked upon directly.

facets.facet_name: Great Spirit — The Cosmic Spiritual Force
facets.core_claim: Gitchi Manitou is not anthropomorphic — the great mystery that animates all reality.

facets.facet_name: The Cannibal Ice Spirit — Greed Personified
facets.core_claim: The Windigo embodies the destructive force of consumption that cannot be satisfied — selfishness as cosmic threat.
Manabozho/Nanabozho creates and remakes the world — Anishinaabe culture hero who brings fire and recreates earth from a grain of sand after the flood.
Ngenechen is simultaneously elder couple and young couple — the supreme deity as the totality of generations.
The pillan are the volcanic ancestors — their power shakes the earth and their voice is thunder.

facets.facet_name: Mother Goddess Emerging from the Sacred Lake
facets.core_claim: Bachué's emergence from Lake Iguaque is the Muisca creation — water as the source of life. She returns to water as serpent at the end.

facets.facet_name: Light Contained Within Himself, Released to Create
facets.core_claim: Chiminigagua contained all light within himself before creation — released, it became the visible cosmos.

facets.facet_name: Akkadian Queen of Love and War
