facets.core_claim: Ishtar inherits Inanna's love-war combination and intensifies it — patron of imperial Assyrian conquest and erotic passion.
Ashur was elevated from city deity to imperial supreme god — divinity following imperial ambition.

facets.facet_name: Divine Scribe and God of Writing
facets.core_claim: Nabu records all human deeds and divine decrees — the Akkadian parallel of Thoth.

facets.facet_name: Night-Demoness Who Steals Children and Seduces Men
facets.core_claim: Lilith haunts the night, steals infants, and seduces sleepers — the feminine adversary in Mesopotamian and Jewish tradition.

facets.facet_name: The First Wife Who Refused Submission
facets.core_claim: In the Alphabet of Ben Sira, Lilith refuses to lie beneath Adam, claims equality, flees Eden. She is the divine feminine who chose autonomy over partnership.

facets.facet_name: The Universal Goddess of Many Names
facets.core_claim: "I am Nature, the Universal Mother... whose single godhead is revered all over the world under different forms, with various rites, by many names" (Apuleius). Isis became the cosmic feminine of late antiquity.

facets.facet_name: Mistress of Magic — Words of Power
facets.core_claim: Isis tricked Ra into revealing his secret name — she possesses all the words of power. The supreme magician of the ancient world.

facets.facet_name: As Above, So Below — Hermetic Cosmology
facets.core_claim: "That which is above is from that which is below, and that which is below is from that which is above" (Emerald Tablet) — the foundational Hermetic principle of cosmic correspondence.

facets.facet_name: Wrathful Female Protector of the Dharma
facets.core_claim: Palden Lhamo's wrath defends what is sacred — protective fury, not destruction.

Bodhidharma transmitted "a special transmission outside the scriptures, not depending on words and letters; directly pointing at the human mind; seeing into one's own nature." The founding statement of Zen.
Hui-Neng demonstrates that enlightenment is not gradual or learned — it can flash in an instant for anyone. The Buddha-nature is already present.

facets.facet_name: The Mandate of Heaven — Cosmic Moral Order
facets.core_claim: Tian is not personal — the cosmic moral order itself, which legitimizes virtuous rulers and removes legitimacy from corrupt ones.

facets.facet_name: The Sage Elevated to Divine Status
facets.core_claim: Confucius's deification by successive Chinese dynasties is the most successful philosopher-to-god transformation in history.

facets.facet_name: Explicit Synthesis of World Religious Traditions
facets.core_claim: Caodaism is the most explicit attempt to synthesize world religious traditions into a single revealed tradition — Buddha, Christ, Muhammad, Lao Tzu, Confucius all aspects of one Supreme.
Samshin protects mothers and infants — the divine grandmother principle in Korean folk religion.

facets.facet_name: The Seven Stars That Govern Destiny
facets.core_claim: The Big Dipper stars govern longevity and fate in Korean folk theology — astral powers shaping human life.
Ongon are the protective spirits of departed shamans — they intercede for the living through the household shrine.

facets.facet_name: Supreme Buryat Sky Father — 99 Sky Beings
facets.core_claim: Eseghe Malan rules 99 tenger split between 55 good (western) and 44 evil (eastern) — explicit cosmic dualism.
Ekhe Burkhan is the feminine source in Buryat shamanism — the great mother of the Siberian steppe.

facets.facet_name: Magyar Sky God — Survived Through Christianization
facets.core_claim: Isten is the pre-Christian Magyar supreme deity — the name continued as the Hungarian word for God after conversion.

facets.facet_name: Ryukyuan Goddess Who Created the Islands
facets.core_claim: Amamikyu created the Ryukyuan archipelago — the matriarchal divine source of Okinawan tradition.

facets.facet_name: Yoruba Orisha Fully Fused with Catholic Saint
facets.core_claim: Changó in Cuba IS Santa Bárbara — the Yoruba orisha and the Catholic saint are not analogous but identical in popular practice.

facets.facet_name: The Premature Rebel Whose Sin Contaminates Creation
facets.core_claim: Ogo's impatience to be born before completion contaminates the cosmos — the Dogon explanation for evil. His descendants (foxes) still reveal hidden truth through divination tracks.

facets.facet_name: Ancestor Twins Who Shaped the Desert
facets.core_claim: The Wati Kutjara walked the desert creating its features — every landscape feature is a record of their journey.

facets.facet_name: The Minoan Mistress of Life and Death
facets.core_claim: The Snake Goddess governs the chthonic powers of birth, death, and rebirth — the snake as the symbol of these cyclical powers.
Aramazd is the Armenian transformation of Persian Ahura Mazda — supreme creator in a Hellenized form.
Anahit was the most worshipped Armenian goddess — her statue at Eriza was one of the most famous in the ancient world.

facets.facet_name: Supreme Sky God of the Georgians
facets.core_claim: Ghmerti is the supreme withdrawn deity — too transcendent for direct contact, accessible only through mediating spirits.
Vidye Mukulu is the apophatic supreme — too sacred for direct image or address.

facets.facet_name: The Female Vessel of Sacred Kingship
facets.core_claim: The Mwadi receives the deceased king's spirit — feminine mediation of masculine divine kingship.

facets.facet_name: The Good Twin Who Creates Beneficial Things
facets.core_claim: Sapling creates everything beneficial — the Iroquois cosmology grounds the world's ambiguity in the dual creation by twins.

facets.facet_name: The Evil Twin Whose Creations Remain
facets.core_claim: Flint creates monsters and thorns by tearing through his mother — his defeat does not erase his works. Evil persists as part of the cosmos.
Huitzilopochtli is the sun-warrior — the cosmos depends on his daily victory over darkness, which depends on the human heart-sacrifice fuel.
Huitzilopochtli led the Mexica from Aztlan to Tenochtitlan — his eagle on the cactus was the sign of their destined city. The Aztec founding god.
Coyolxauhqui's dismemberment by her newborn brother explains the moon's waning — fratricidal cosmic violence.
Itzpapalotl is the divine feminine in her most terrifying form — beauty (butterfly) made deadly (obsidian).

facets.facet_name: Moche Moon as Supreme Celestial Power
facets.core_claim: For the Moche, the moon (Si) was more powerful than the sun — a coastal Andean alternative to highland solar supremacy.
The Olmec Dragon is the earliest documented Mesoamerican divine being — the structural ancestor of Quetzalcoatl and Kukulkan.

facets.facet_name: Fish Avatar — Savior from the Cosmic Flood
facets.core_claim: Matsya warns Manu of the coming flood and pulls his boat to safety — the Hindu parallel of Noah and Utnapishtim.
Kurma supports Mount Mandara during the churning — the cosmic foundation beneath all creative effort.

facets.facet_name: Boar Avatar — Lifting Earth from the Cosmic Sea
facets.core_claim: Varaha dives into the cosmic ocean to rescue the earth — restoring cosmic order through descent and return.

Narasimha kills Hiranyakashipu by being neither man nor animal, killing him neither inside nor outside, neither during day nor night — divine power working through paradox to circumvent invincibility.

facets.facet_name: The Dwarf Whose Three Paces Cover the Cosmos
facets.core_claim: Vamana asks for three paces of land then expands to cover all reality — the trickster avatar.

facets.facet_name: The Wrathful Brahmin — Destroyer of Corrupt Kings
facets.core_claim: Parashurama destroys corrupt Kshatriya rulers 21 times — divine wrath against systemic injustice.

facets.facet_name: The Ideal King — Embodiment of Dharma
facets.core_claim: Rama embodies perfect dharma — virtuous king, perfect husband, defender of cosmic order. The Hindu ideal of righteous kingship.
In Vaishnava tradition, the Buddha is incorporated as the 9th avatar — but described as sent to mislead the unworthy. The most theologically charged syncretic move in Hinduism.
Lakshmi blesses with material and spiritual abundance — fickle but never absent from the divine.
Lakshmi and Vishnu are the inseparable divine couple — masculine preserving order and feminine providing abundance.

facets.facet_name: Goddess of Wisdom, Music, and Speech
facets.core_claim: Saraswati is the divine principle of articulated wisdom — speech, music, and learning all flow from her.

facets.facet_name: The Divine Spouse — Erotic Counterpart of Shiva
facets.core_claim: Parvati is the divine feminine in her partnered form — without her, Shiva is inert ascetic; with her, the cosmos generates.
Parvati is the daughter of the Himalaya — the divine feminine grounded in mountain power.
Kartikeya leads the divine army — youth, victory, and the strength to defend cosmic order.
Hanuman demonstrates that perfect devotion (bhakti) is itself supernatural power — the servant who is greater than kings.
Vayu is the wind outside and the breath within — the same divine principle in cosmic and personal scale.

facets.facet_name: The Boundless — Mother of the Great Gods
facets.core_claim: Aditi is unbounded space-feminine — the cosmic womb from which the Adityas emerge.

facets.facet_name: The Active Feminine Energy of the Cosmos
facets.core_claim: Shakti is the active cosmic energy — without her, the masculine divine is inert. The feminine as the principle of activity itself.

facets.facet_name: The Preserver Who Descends as Avatars
facets.core_claim: Vishnu preserves cosmic order — when dharma declines, he descends as an avatar to restore it.

facets.facet_name: Reclining on the Cosmic Serpent — Between Creations
facets.core_claim: Vishnu reclines on Shesha (the cosmic serpent) on the milk ocean between universes — the divine in cosmic stillness between creations.
Brahma creates the universe — his four faces see all four directions. The least worshipped creator god in any major religion despite being THE creator.
Gautama Buddha did not claim to be divine — he claimed to have awakened from the dream of ego. His path is for all beings.

facets.facet_name: Teacher of the Dharma — 45 Years of Teaching
facets.core_claim: The Buddha taught the Dharma for 45 years — his teachings (sutras) form the foundation of Buddhism. He set the wheel of Dharma turning.

facets.facet_name: The Diamond Being — Purifier of All Karma
facets.core_claim: Vajrasattva's 100-syllable mantra purifies all karmic obscurations — the Vajrayana practice of inner cleansing.

facets.facet_name: The Destroyer of Death — Wrathful Manjushri
facets.core_claim: Yamantaka defeats Yama (Death) — wisdom in wrathful form, destroying the prison of mortality.
White Tara's seven eyes see suffering everywhere — her compassion brings healing and long life.

facets.facet_name: The Black Protector of the Dharma
facets.core_claim: Mahakala's wrath defends the Dharma — Hindu Shiva transformed into Buddhist protector.

facets.facet_name: Tantric Union — Wisdom and Method Inseparable
facets.core_claim: Hevajra in union with Nairatmya represents the inseparable union of wisdom (prajna) and method (upaya) — the highest tantric realization.

facets.facet_name: The Bound Trickster Whose Children End the World
facets.core_claim: Loki's mischief turned to malice — he engineered Baldur's death and is now bound beneath the earth, awaiting Ragnarok.
Loki gives birth to Sleipnir (Odin's 8-legged horse) while in mare form — gender-fluid trickster supremacy.

facets.facet_name: God of Sea, Trade, and Wealth from the Sea
facets.core_claim: Njord governs the sea and its bounty — wealth flowing from peaceful seafaring.

facets.facet_name: One-Handed God of Oaths and Lawful Combat
facets.core_claim: Tyr sacrificed his hand to bind Fenrir — the god who keeps oaths even when it costs him.
Tyr/Tiwaz was the original Germanic *Dyeus before Odin displaced him — Tuesday (Tiw's day) preserves his memory.

facets.facet_name: Keeper of the Apples of Immortality
facets.core_claim: Idun's apples maintain the gods' youth — without her, even the gods age and die.

facets.facet_name: The Three Norns Who Weave All Fate
facets.core_claim: The Norns weave the fate of all beings — their decrees override even the gods. Past, present, and future as three weavers.

facets.facet_name: Judge of the Dead at the Weighing of the Heart
facets.core_claim: Anubis weighs each heart against Ma'at's feather — the cosmic judge of moral worthiness.

facets.facet_name: Divine Embalmer Who Prepares the Dead
facets.core_claim: Anubis taught the Egyptians embalming — death is not an end but a transition requiring divine craft.

facets.facet_name: Dismembered God — Body Scattered Across the World
facets.core_claim: Osiris's body was cut into 14 pieces and scattered — each piece became a sacred place in Egypt. His body literally IS the sacred geography.
Apophis attacks Ra each night — the principle of non-being that the cosmos must defeat to continue existing.

facets.facet_name: The Feather Against Which All Hearts Are Weighed
facets.core_claim: Every heart is weighed against Ma'at's feather — the moral cosmos rests on this measurement.
Hera is the queen — her jealousy is the divine principle defending sacred marriage against violation.
Hades rules an irreversible domain — not malevolent but absolute. Plouton (wealth) refers to the underground riches.

facets.facet_name: God of Ecstatic Dissolution of the Self
facets.core_claim: Dionysus dissolves the boundaries of self — his rites grant divine experience through wine, dance, and ritual frenzy.

facets.facet_name: God of Wine and Sacred Intoxication
facets.core_claim: Wine as the gateway to divine experience — Dionysus IS the wine.

facets.facet_name: The Fire Theft — Divine Knowledge for Humanity
facets.core_claim: Prometheus steals fire (divine knowledge) for humanity at the cost of eternal torment. The archetype of the rebel who suffers for human progress.

facets.facet_name: The Singer Who Almost Recovered His Beloved from Death
facets.core_claim: Orpheus's song moves even Hades — but he loses Eurydice by looking back. The hero who almost defeated death.

facets.facet_name: Founder of the Orphic Mystery Tradition
facets.core_claim: Orphic theology of the soul's descent and liberation traces to Orpheus — the proto-Gnostic Greek mystery.

facets.facet_name: The Three Fates Who Bind Even the Gods
facets.core_claim: Clotho spins, Lachesis measures, Atropos cuts — even Zeus cannot override their decree.

facets.facet_name: The Divine Champion Who Achieved Godhood Through Labor
facets.core_claim: Herakles's 12 labors are the archetype of the hero's redemptive journey — divinity achieved through suffering.

facets.facet_name: The Primordial Being Whose Body Becomes the World
facets.core_claim: Pangu's death-becoming-cosmos is the Chinese cosmogony of body-into-world — his eyes are sun and moon, his breath is wind.

facets.facet_name: The Mother of the Ten Suns
facets.core_claim: Xi He birthed the ten suns — solar feminine in pre-classical Chinese cosmology.

facets.facet_name: The Archer Who Saved the World from Burning
facets.core_claim: Houyi's arrows save the world from the ten simultaneous suns — civilization survives by his cosmic marksmanship.
Chang'e on the moon with her rabbit — the most beloved figure in Chinese popular religion.
The Eight Immortals each represent a different way of achieving immortality — collectively a complete map of Taoist spiritual paths.

facets.facet_name: The Nine-Tailed Fox — Auspicious or Demonic
facets.core_claim: The nine-tailed fox is the most ambiguous Chinese mythological being — divine omen and seductress demon simultaneously.

facets.facet_name: The Monkey King — Trickster of Heaven
facets.core_claim: Sun Wukong defeats all the gods of heaven before being humbled by Buddha — the trickster who finally finds his place in cosmic order.

facets.facet_name: The Cultural Hero Who Tamed the Floods
facets.core_claim: Yu the Great's taming of the rivers founded Chinese civilization — the cosmic order required active human partnership.

facets.facet_name: Vedic Dawn Goddess — Purest PIE Survival
facets.core_claim: Ushas is the dawn — she returns every morning, the supreme PIE goddess whose name survives in English Easter.

facets.facet_name: The First Man — Father of Humanity
facets.core_claim: Manu is the PIE first man — survived the flood, fathered all humanity. The Indo-European Adam.

facets.facet_name: Chief of the Gods — Cauldron, Club, and Harp
facets.core_claim: The Dagda's three great tools govern life, death, and time — the Irish supreme father deity.

facets.facet_name: The Battle-Trance — Hero Transformed by War
facets.core_claim: Cu Chulainn's riastrad is the most vivid battle-trance in IE literature.

facets.facet_name: Primordial Chaos-King with the Destroying Eye
facets.core_claim: Balor embodies Fomorian chaos — his eye destroys all it sees.

facets.facet_name: King with the Silver Arm — Perfect Kingship
facets.core_claim: Nuada must be physically whole to be king — sacred kingship requires embodied completeness.
Dian Cecht heals the gods at the Well of Slaine but kills his own son from jealousy.
Gwydion transforms reality through magic — the Welsh trickster-magician.

facets.facet_name: The Cauldron of Inspiration — Death and Rebirth
facets.core_claim: Ceridwen's cauldron brews the Awen (divine inspiration) — the accidental drinker is reborn as the supreme bard.

facets.facet_name: The Wanderer Who Sacrificed Everything for Wisdom
facets.core_claim: Odin sacrificed his eye for wisdom at Mimir's Well, then hung on Yggdrasil for nine days to win the runes — the price of divine knowledge is total self-abnegation.

facets.facet_name: Hung on Yggdrasil — Self-Sacrifice for Runes
facets.core_claim: "I know that I hung on the windswept tree nine full nights, wounded by a spear, given to Odin — myself to myself." (Havamal 138)

facets.facet_name: Master of the Runes — Cosmic Writing System
facets.core_claim: Odin discovered the runes through his self-sacrifice — they are the divine alphabet that shapes reality.
Thor is the defender of humanity and Midgard — he kills giants and serpents to protect the world.

facets.facet_name: God of Sunshine, Rain, and Abundance
facets.core_claim: Freyr brings the sunshine and rain that grow the crops — the Vanir deity of agricultural life.

facets.facet_name: The All-Hearing Guardian of the Gods
facets.core_claim: Heimdall guards Bifrost — his senses are superhuman; his horn signals the end of the world.

facets.facet_name: The World Tree Connecting All Nine Realms
facets.core_claim: Yggdrasil connects all nine Norse worlds — the axis of the cosmos, source of wisdom, and site of Odin's sacrifice.

facets.facet_name: The Bound Wolf Whose Release Ends the World
facets.core_claim: Fenrir is bound until Ragnarok — the inevitable comes. He will swallow Odin.
Skadi skis across the frozen north — goddess of the independent hunter and winter wilderness.

facets.facet_name: The First Imam — Gate of Divine Knowledge
facets.core_claim: Ali is the first Imam — the inheritor of the Prophet's esoteric knowledge. Shia Islam's divine mediator.

facets.facet_name: The Martyrdom at Karbala — Cosmic Sacrifice
facets.core_claim: Husayn's death at Karbala is the redemptive tragedy at the center of Shia consciousness — the righteous who dies for truth.

facets.facet_name: The Radiant Lady — Intercessor and Cosmic Figure
facets.core_claim: Fatimah's cosmic status in Shia Islam parallels Mary's in Catholic theology — the divine feminine intercessor.

facets.facet_name: The Living Imam — Always-Present Divine Guide
facets.core_claim: The Nizari Imam is always present and accessible — the living Word guiding the community.

facets.facet_name: The Slain Lamb Who Rules the Cosmos
facets.core_claim: The paradox of Revelation: the slaughtered lamb is the ruler of history.

facets.facet_name: The Anti-Divine Feminine of the Apocalypse
facets.core_claim: The Whore of Babylon is the dark mirror of the New Jerusalem — imperial corruption as cosmic adversary.

facets.facet_name: The Perfect City Descending from Heaven
facets.core_claim: The New Jerusalem is both a city and a bride — the perfect divine society at the end of history.
Amida's grace is already given — even once calling his name with sincerity guarantees liberation.
Shinran's synthesis: even faith is Amida's gift; gratitude, not earning, is the human response.

facets.facet_name: The Sage Who Flows with the Tao
facets.core_claim: Zhuangzi dreamt he was a butterfly — woke and did not know: was he a man who dreamt of being a butterfly, or now a butterfly dreaming of being a man?

facets.facet_name: Primal Chaos Killed by Good Intentions
facets.core_claim: Hundun dies when given the seven senses — perfection through non-interference.

facets.facet_name: The Tantric Union That Is Liberation
facets.core_claim: Chakrasamvara in union with Vajravarahi embodies the inseparability of bliss and emptiness.
Vajrayogini is the most secret and advanced Vajrayana practice — the divine feminine as the direct path to enlightenment.

facets.facet_name: Lord of Body, Speech, and Mind
facets.core_claim: Guhyasamaja governs the three mysteries (body, speech, mind) — the complete map of sentient existence.

facets.facet_name: The Supreme Igbo Creator — Great Chi
facets.core_claim: Chukwu is beyond direct approach — the great chi behind all lesser chi.

facets.facet_name: Earth Goddess Who Is Also the Moral Law
facets.core_claim: Ala is the earth and the foundation of all Igbo ethics — her laws define what is sacred and what is taboo.
Amadioha's lightning strike is a divine verdict — the loudest moral statement in the sky.
Ifejioku presides over the yam — the Igbo staff of life.

facets.facet_name: Earth Mother Whose Rest Day Is Thursday
facets.core_claim: Asase Yaa IS the earth — farming on Thursday would wound her body.

facets.facet_name: The Priest Who Called the Soul of the Nation
facets.core_claim: Okomfo Anokye called the Golden Stool from heaven — the stool contains the Ashanti national soul.
Cocijo is the Zapotec storm deity — pre-Aztec Mesoamerican rain worship in its southern expression.
The corn god across Mesoamerica — sustaining life through the sacred plant.

facets.facet_name: The Historical Priest-King Elevated to Myth
facets.core_claim: The historical Quetzalcoatl merged with the divine feathered serpent — human greatness absorbed into cosmic myth.

facets.facet_name: Supreme Creator of the Crow People
facets.core_claim: Akbaatatdia creates and sustains — the withdrawn Crow sky creator.
Old Man Coyote uses the earth-diver pattern to remake the world — the Crow trickster.

facets.facet_name: Creator of Human Bodies in Candomble
facets.core_claim: Oxalá creates the human body — the divine sculptor who gives form to each soul.

facets.facet_name: Queen of the Sea — Brazilian Great Mother
facets.core_claim: Iemanjá is the most widely celebrated Brazilian religious figure — the sea mother who receives prayers and flowers each February.

facets.facet_name: Brazilian Goddess of Love and Fresh Water
facets.core_claim: Oxum governs love and sweetness — the fresh water that sustains life as opposed to Iemanjá's salt sea.

facets.facet_name: Khakass Sky Father in a 16+16 Cosmos
facets.core_claim: Khakass cosmology structures reality into 16 heavenly and 16 underworld levels — among the most elaborated vertical cosmos.

facets.facet_name: The White Creator Lord of the Eastern Heaven
facets.core_claim: Ai Tojon is the supreme benevolent creator of Yakut shamanism — light and goodness from the highest east.
For Ibn Arabi, there is only Al-Haqq — everything is God knowing itself through its own attributes.
Al-Farabi maps Plotinus's Nous onto Islamic emanationism — the bridge between Greek and Islamic metaphysics.
En no Gyoja opened the mountains as sacred space — the Japanese mountain ascetic tradition.
Gongen is the syncretic fusion of native kami and Buddhist deity — Japan's theological synthesis.
Ine-Pas is the supreme Mordvin deity — the Uralic sky god in his Volga region form.

facets.facet_name: The Lwa of Love and Impossible Longing
facets.core_claim: Erzulie weeps because no love in the world can satisfy her divine longing — she is love itself in an imperfect world.

facets.facet_name: The Rainbow Serpent Who Supports the Sky
facets.core_claim: Damballa is the oldest lwa — the primordial serpent whose coils support the sky.

facets.facet_name: Spirit of Iron and Political Liberation
facets.core_claim: Ogou carried the Haitian Revolution — iron wielded for freedom rather than colonial chains.

facets.facet_name: The Cosmic Serpent Whose Body IS Pattern
facets.core_claim: Ronin's body generates the geometric patterns that structure visible reality — the cosmos as divine serpent-design.

facets.facet_name: Sacred Plant Spirit and Gateway to the Divine
facets.core_claim: Jurema is the plant whose spirit enables direct contact with the divine world.

facets.facet_name: Ifa Divination Oracle in Cuban Diaspora
facets.core_claim: Orula carried the Ifa oracle through the Middle Passage — his wisdom survived diaspora.

facets.facet_name: Creator of Humanity in Afro-Cuban Tradition
facets.core_claim: Obatala creates in Cuban Lucumi as in Yoruba — the divine sculptor adapted to diaspora.

facets.facet_name: The Shadow Sister — Hidden Counterpart of Isis
facets.core_claim: Nephthys is Isis's dark twin — where Isis is visible motherhood, Nephthys is hidden mourning.
Sobek embodies the Nile's dual nature — giver of fertility and sudden death.

facets.facet_name: The Ram-Potter Who Shapes Body and Soul
facets.core_claim: Khnum shapes each human body and its spiritual double on his potter's wheel at the moment of conception.

facets.facet_name: The Original War Falcon of Thebes
facets.core_claim: Montu embodies pure martial ferocity — the military power of the pharaoh made divine.

facets.facet_name: The Moon Goddess Who Drives the Night
facets.core_claim: Selene IS the moon — her chariot crossing the sky IS the moon moving.

facets.facet_name: The Sun God Who IS the Solar Chariot
facets.core_claim: Helios drives the sun as a chariot — the most literal solar deity in Greek tradition.

facets.facet_name: Divine Victory — Power That Gives Wings
facets.core_claim: Nike embodies the divine favor of victory — every Athenian victory had her presence.

facets.facet_name: Divine Healer Through Dream and Snake
facets.core_claim: Asclepius heals through dream-incubation — sleeping in his temple, the god appears and cures.

facets.facet_name: The Greek Dawn Goddess — Opening Heaven's Gates
facets.core_claim: Eos opens heaven for the sun — she IS the dawn.
Ananke is deeper than the gods — necessity binds even Zeus. Plato's Spindle holds the cosmos.

facets.facet_name: Roman Queen of Gods and Guardian of the State
facets.core_claim: Juno is the state as divine feminine — her protection IS Roman civic order.
Minerva governs the full spectrum of human intelligence — art, craft, medicine, and strategy.
Bacchus was both feared (Senate suppressed his cult) and essential — wine as divine madness.
Pluto is generous — the underground riches of earth belong to him.
Neptune governs all waters — sea, springs, rivers, and horses.

facets.facet_name: Roman God of Commerce and the Most Syncretized Deity
facets.core_claim: Mercury was identified with more foreign gods than any other Roman deity — the universal principle of exchange.

facets.facet_name: Roman Grain Mother and Plebeian Goddess
facets.core_claim: Ceres is grain itself — her name survives in cereal. The plebeian counterpart to aristocratic Juno.
Brahma creates the universe but has almost no temples — the creator is less important than the preserver and destroyer.

facets.facet_name: Dwells in Tushita Heaven Awaiting the Right Time
facets.core_claim: Maitreya waits in Tushita until conditions ripen — the divine who knows when to act.
Shekhinah is God's presence in the world — the divine feminine face of the transcendent male God.

facets.facet_name: The Individual Soul IS the Cosmic Soul
facets.core_claim: "Tat tvam asi" (That art thou) — the deepest Upanishadic insight: there is no separation between self and ultimate reality.

facets.facet_name: Emptiness as the Ground of All Phenomena
facets.core_claim: Shunyata (emptiness) is not nihilism — it is the open ground from which all phenomena arise, unbounded by fixed essence.

facets.facet_name: Cosmic Law — The Way Things Are
facets.core_claim: Dharma is the cosmic law that maintains reality — in Hinduism the moral-social order, in Buddhism the truth of things.
Asha is the Zoroastrian cosmic truth — the principle that upholds reality against the lie (druj).

facets.facet_name: Her Death Begins the Agricultural Calendar
facets.core_claim: Selu's sacrifice initiated the Cherokee agricultural cycle — her death is the origin of every harvest.

facets.facet_name: The Web as Map of All Connections
facets.core_claim: Anansi's web connects everything — his stories are the connective tissue of reality.

facets.facet_name: The Storm God Who Brings the Life-Giving Rains
facets.core_claim: Baal brings rain to Canaan — his defeat of Yam (sea) and Mot (death) makes agricultural life possible.

facets.facet_name: Queen of Heaven and All Her Domains
facets.core_claim: Inanna is queen of heaven, earth, and the underworld — she spans all three cosmic levels.

facets.facet_name: The Bond with Enkidu — Friendship as Transformation
facets.core_claim: Gilgamesh is transformed by his friendship with Enkidu — and broken by his death. The oldest literary treatment of grief.

facets.facet_name: Divine Ancestor of the Japanese Imperial Line
facets.core_claim: Every Japanese emperor is descended from Amaterasu — the imperial family's divine legitimacy.

facets.facet_name: Slayer of the Python — Establishing the Oracle
facets.core_claim: Apollo killed the Python and claimed Delphi — establishing the most important oracle in the ancient world.

facets.facet_name: Inventor of Language, Music, and Divination
facets.core_claim: On his birth-day, Hermes invented the lyre, fire-sticks, and cattle-tracking — divine intelligence expressed through invention.
The eagle is Zeus's sacred bird and his form — sky sovereignty expressed through the king of birds.
Medusa was once beautiful; her power-that-kills is the dark face of divine feminine energy.

facets.facet_name: Queen Who Initiates Souls at Eleusis
facets.core_claim: Persephone presides over the Eleusinian Mysteries — promising initiates a blessed afterlife.

facets.facet_name: The Creator Couple Who Made Japan by Sexual Union
facets.core_claim: Izanagi and Izanami together make the islands by sexual union — the cosmos born from divine marriage.

facets.facet_name: The Weaver Goddess of Heavenly Cloth
facets.core_claim: Amaterasu weaves the fabric of heaven — the universe as divine textile.
The Jade Emperor's celestial bureau records every human being's fate — cosmic administration as divine care.

facets.facet_name: God of Both War and Righteous Commerce
facets.core_claim: Guan Yu is worshipped by both soldiers and merchants — righteousness is the link between them.

facets.facet_name: Author of the Way That Can Be Spoken Is Not the Eternal Way
facets.core_claim: "The Tao that can be named is not the eternal Tao" — the foundational apophatic statement of Taoism.

facets.facet_name: The Deified Royal Ancestor Who Became Thunder
facets.core_claim: Shango was the fourth Alaafin of Oyo — his deification shows royal power becoming cosmic force.

facets.facet_name: First to Clear the Path — Iron Before Everything
facets.core_claim: Ogun cuts the first path through the primordial bush — iron as the prerequisite for civilization.

facets.facet_name: Lord of All Fish and Sea Creatures
facets.core_claim: Tangaroa's children are the fish — all sea life is his family.

facets.facet_name: Snared the Sun to Lengthen the Day
facets.core_claim: Maui lassos the sun with his grandmother's jawbone and beats it slow — the days grow longer.

facets.facet_name: The Sky Father Who Fecundates the Earth
facets.core_claim: The PIE sky-father fecundates the earth-mother — rain as the cosmic marriage.

facets.facet_name: The Spirit That Hovers Over the Waters
facets.core_claim: "The Spirit of God moved upon the face of the waters" (Genesis 1:2) — the divine breath before creation.

facets.facet_name: The Prophet Taken to Heaven in a Fiery Chariot
facets.core_claim: Elijah ascends in a chariot of fire — the Hebrew prophet who never died, expected to return.

facets.facet_name: The Demon King Who Was Also a Devotee of Shiva
facets.core_claim: Ravana abducted Sita and challenged Rama — but was also the greatest devotee of Shiva. The adversary as a complex theological figure.
Every Roman household had its Lares (ancestral spirits) and Penates (pantry gods) — the divine in the domestic.

facets.facet_name: Rita — The Vedic Cosmic Truth Order
facets.core_claim: Rita is the Vedic cosmic truth from which everything flows — the same principle as Avestan Asha, Egyptian Maat.
Osiris's green skin marks him as the grain — his annual death and resurrection IS the agricultural cycle.
Osiris presides as judge in the Hall of Two Truths — the dead declare what they have NOT done wrong.

facets.facet_name: Tricked Ra to Learn His Secret Name
facets.core_claim: Isis made Ra sick, then offered to heal him only if he revealed his secret name — she won all his power.

facets.facet_name: As a Kite, She Gathered the Pieces of Osiris
facets.core_claim: Isis took the form of a kite to fly over Egypt gathering Osiris's dismembered pieces — divine love as physical search.

facets.facet_name: The Linga — The Formless Absolute as Pillar of Light
facets.core_claim: The Shiva linga is not phallic but the cosmic pillar of light — Shiva appeared as a column of fire stretching to infinity, humbling Brahma and Vishnu.

facets.facet_name: Rudra — the Wild Storm God of the Vedas
facets.core_claim: Shiva's Vedic form Rudra is the howling storm who brings both disease and healing — the wild god outside the sacrificial system.
In Shaivism, Shiva is the supreme deity — Vishnu and Brahma are his manifestations, not his equals.

facets.facet_name: Reclining on the Cosmic Serpent Between Creations
facets.core_claim: Vishnu rests on Anantashesha (infinite serpent) on the milk ocean — the divine at rest between universes.

facets.facet_name: The Sudarshana Chakra — Weapon of Divine Will
facets.core_claim: Vishnu's spinning chakra destroys whatever opposes dharma — divine will as an unstoppable force.

facets.facet_name: Goddess of Heavenly Textile — The Universe as Woven Cloth
facets.core_claim: Amaterasu weaves the fabric of heaven — the cosmos as divine cloth, creation as weaving.

facets.facet_name: First Farmer of the Heavenly Rice Paddy
facets.core_claim: Amaterasu cultivated the first rice in the heavens — agricultural civilization is her gift.

facets.facet_name: Feathered Serpent — Sky and Earth United
facets.core_claim: The feathered serpent combines sky (feathers/bird) and earth (serpent) in one form — the cosmic mediator.
Quetzalcoatl is the morning star — Venus as the cosmic herald who dies into the underworld and rises.

facets.facet_name: First Oracle at Delphi — Before Apollo
facets.core_claim: Delphi was Gaia's oracle before Apollo killed the Python and claimed it — the earth spoke prophecy first.

facets.facet_name: Generates the Titans as Revolt Against Uranos
facets.core_claim: Gaia gives Kronos the sickle to castrate Uranos — she uses her children as instruments of justice.
Zeus knows his son Sarpedon must die at Troy — he cannot save him from Fate. The king of gods is not omnipotent.

facets.facet_name: Zeus Takes Every Form — Swan, Bull, Gold, Eagle
facets.core_claim: Zeus's many disguised loves generate the demigods and heroes who shape Greek civilization.

facets.facet_name: Merged with Helios as Solar Deity in Late Antiquity
facets.core_claim: Apollo was not originally solar — he absorbed Helios's solar attributes in Hellenistic and Roman period.

facets.facet_name: Sender of Plague as Well as Healer
facets.core_claim: In the Iliad's opening, Apollo sends plague on the Greeks — the same god sends and heals disease.

facets.facet_name: Dakshina Kali — the Most Accessible Form
facets.core_claim: Dakshina Kali is the common form — standing on Shiva's chest, tongue out, removing fear.

facets.facet_name: Lady of the Cremation Ground — Destroyer of Ego
facets.core_claim: Kali dances in the cremation ground — where ego dissolves, liberation begins.

facets.facet_name: Nine Nights of Durga — the Annual Battle
facets.core_claim: Navratri celebrates Durga's nine-night battle against Mahishasura — good and evil war annually.

facets.facet_name: His Elephant Head — Beheaded and Restored by Shiva
facets.core_claim: Shiva beheaded Ganesha then restored him with an elephant's head — divine paradox at birth.

facets.facet_name: His Body Cannot Be Destroyed — Vayu's Wind-Son
facets.core_claim: Hanuman's body is indestructible — he is literally the son of the wind.

facets.facet_name: He Bears Rama's Name on His Heart
facets.core_claim: Hanuman tore open his chest to reveal Rama's name written on his heart — devotion made literal.

facets.facet_name: The Four Sights That Ended the Princes Life
facets.core_claim: Sick man, old man, corpse, wandering ascetic — these four visions ended the prince's sheltered life.
The Buddha's enlightenment is the most cosmically significant event in Buddhism — the axle around which the Dharma turns.

facets.facet_name: The Allfather — Father of Gods and Men
facets.core_claim: Odin is father of the gods and of humanity (created Ask and Embla) — the primal divine parent.

facets.facet_name: Commands the Valkyries — Choosers of the Slain
facets.core_claim: Odin's valkyries choose who dies in battle and who goes to Valhalla — death as divine selection.

facets.facet_name: Stole the Mead of Poetry from the Giants
facets.core_claim: Odin seduced Gunnlod and stole the Mead of Poetry — giving the gift of poetic inspiration to gods and chosen humans.

facets.facet_name: His Goats Are Killed and Resurrected Each Night
facets.core_claim: Thor's goats are eaten each night and resurrected from their bones each morning — the daily regeneration.
The oak is sacred to Thor — Europe's strongest tree for the storm god.

facets.facet_name: Her Tears Are Gold and Amber
facets.core_claim: When Freya weeps for Odr, her tears become gold — divine grief generates precious substance.
Freya's sacred boar connects her to the earth's fecundity and the ferocity of battle.
Loki fathered Fenrir (who kills Odin), Jormungandr (who kills Thor), and Hel (who rules the dead) — the adversary's children ARE the Ragnarok.

facets.facet_name: The Thunderbolt as Instrument of Justice
facets.core_claim: Zeus's thunderbolt punishes hubris and injustice — cosmic power in the service of moral order.
Demeter founded the Eleusinian Mysteries at Eleusis — promising initiates a blessed afterlife.

facets.facet_name: Born Fully Armored from Zeus's Head
facets.core_claim: Athena leaps fully armored from Zeus's head — divine wisdom is the direct product of the father-mind.
Every four years, Athens wove a new robe for Athena's statue — the city weaving its relationship with divine wisdom.

facets.facet_name: The Caduceus — Two Serpents on a Staff
facets.core_claim: Two serpents on a staff — the caduceus is the symbol of commerce, medicine, and divine communication.

facets.facet_name: The Lame God — Rejection and Mastery
facets.core_claim: Hephaestus is thrown from Olympus and lamed — the greatest craftsman was cast out and marginalized.
Persephone ate six pomegranate seeds — she must spend six months in the underworld each year. The seasons are a divine legal contract.

facets.facet_name: The Twelve Hours of the Underworld Journey
facets.core_claim: Each of Ra's twelve underworld hours is a distinct region — the night journey is the most complete map of the Egyptian afterlife.

facets.facet_name: Atum-Ra Creates First Gods from His Own Substance
facets.core_claim: In the Heliopolitan theology, Atum creates Shu and Tefnut through his own body — self-generating divinity.

facets.facet_name: The Ibis and the Baboon — Two Sacred Forms
facets.core_claim: Thoth appears as ibis (scribal precision) and baboon (observant watchfulness) — two aspects of divine intelligence.

facets.facet_name: Reckoner of Time and Measurer of the Cosmos
facets.core_claim: Thoth measures the hours, days, and years — time itself requires divine intelligence to maintain.

facets.facet_name: The Wedjet Eye — Healing, Protection, Royal Power
facets.core_claim: The Eye of Horus (lost in battle with Set, restored by Thoth) became the supreme protective symbol of Egypt.

facets.facet_name: The 80-Year Divine Trial for the Throne
facets.core_claim: Horus's 80-year legal battle with Set is the Egyptian myth of justice — divine courts determine cosmic right.

facets.facet_name: Lord of the Abzu — the Cosmic Freshwater Deep
facets.core_claim: The abzu is Enki's underwater domain — the source of all fresh water and cosmic wisdom.

facets.facet_name: Keeper of the Me — All Arts of Civilization
facets.core_claim: Enki holds the me — writing, kingship, music, descent to the underworld, ascent from the underworld, lying, truth.

facets.facet_name: His Birthday on the Ninth Day — Jade Emperor Festival
facets.core_claim: The Jade Emperor's birthday (9th of the 1st lunar month) is one of the most important Chinese folk celebrations.
Sun Wukong can transform into 72 different forms — each transformation is a challenge to fixed identity.

facets.facet_name: The Great Sage Equal to Heaven — Divine Pride
facets.core_claim: Sun Wukong claims the title "Great Sage Equal to Heaven" — the audacity that led to his imprisonment.

facets.facet_name: Repaired the Sky with Five-Colored Stones
facets.core_claim: Nuwa melted five-colored stones to patch the broken sky — divine craft maintaining cosmic integrity.

facets.facet_name: As Ehecatl — He Blew Away the Obstacles for the Sun's First Rising
facets.core_claim: Quetzalcoatl as wind god blew hard to clear the path for the first sunrise — creation through breath.

facets.facet_name: Born Mid-Battle from Coatlicue's Bundle of Feathers
facets.core_claim: Huitzilopochtli is born fully armored from a ball of feathers — the war god needs no gestation period.

facets.facet_name: Slowed the Sun with His Grandmother's Jawbone
facets.core_claim: Maui beats the sun until it agrees to move more slowly — longer days for human work and life.

facets.facet_name: His Scales Become Fish, His Bones Become Coral
facets.core_claim: In some traditions, Tangaroa's own body generates the sea's creatures — creation from divine substance.
Raven stole the box containing daylight from the chief who hoarded it — the most famous Northwest Coast story.

facets.facet_name: She Gave the Seven Sacred Rites of the Lakota
facets.core_claim: White Buffalo Calf Woman gave the seven sacred ceremonies that structure Lakota spiritual life.
Agni is present as the domestic hearth fire, the sacrificial altar fire, and the funeral pyre — he spans all human life transitions.

facets.facet_name: Drinks Soma Before Battle — Divine Intoxication
facets.core_claim: Indra drinks vast quantities of soma before his great battles — sacred intoxication as divine power.

facets.facet_name: His Power Gradually Diminished as Vishnu Rose
facets.core_claim: Indra's early supremacy gradually yielded to Vishnu and Shiva — the theological evolution within Hinduism.

facets.facet_name: Hosts the Banquet of Peaches Every 3000 Years
facets.core_claim: Xi Wangmu's peach banquet of the immortals is the Chinese parallel of the Norse Idun's apples.

facets.facet_name: Her Ancient Tiger Form — before She Was Beautified
facets.core_claim: In the earliest texts, Xi Wangmu is a tiger-toothed wild goddess — her refinement into the gracious queen is a theological softening.

facets.facet_name: His Red Face — The Color of Loyalty and War
facets.core_claim: Guan Yu's red face marks him as a man of passion and absolute loyalty — iconography encoding virtue.

facets.facet_name: The White Fox as Divine Messenger
facets.core_claim: The white fox is Inari's messenger and symbol — simultaneously a trickster and a sacred intermediary.

facets.facet_name: Mother of the Eight Million Kami
facets.core_claim: Izanami birthed the deities of Japan including the kami of mountains, rivers, trees, and sea.

facets.facet_name: Created the World by Speaking Divine Words
facets.core_claim: Io spoke the first words into the void and the world came into being — creation by divine utterance.

facets.facet_name: The Po — The Smallest Seed Containing All Existence
facets.core_claim: Amma's creation begins with the po — the infinitesimally small seed that contains all potential.

facets.facet_name: Created the World in Seven Days of Rest and Labor
facets.core_claim: Mawu-Lisa created in alternating days of work and rest — a seven-day creation structurally paralleling Genesis.

facets.facet_name: Gave Cattle to the Maasai — Divine Covenant
facets.core_claim: Engai lowered cattle to the Maasai on a strap of hide — they are Engai's cattle, entrusted to the Maasai.
Ranginui and Papatuanuku were separated by their children — their grief (rain, mist) sustains the world they love.

facets.facet_name: Sent Her Sister Hi'iaka to Fetch Her Lover
facets.core_claim: Pele sends Hi'iaka on a dangerous journey to bring her lover Lohiau — divine love expressed through proxy.

facets.facet_name: Gave the First Laws and Ceremonies to the Wiradjuri
facets.core_claim: Baiame established the first laws and ceremonies — the divine law that structures Wiradjuri social life.
Yaldabaoth's form combines lion (pride and power) with serpent (deception) — the visual theology of the false creator.

facets.facet_name: The Qliphoth — The Dark Mirror of the Sefirot
facets.core_claim: Each Sefirah has its Qliphah (husk/shell) — the demonic counterpart that mirrors the divine in darkness.

facets.facet_name: The Lightning Flash — Emanation from Keter to Malkhut
facets.core_claim: Divine energy flows down the Tree of Life in a lightning-flash pattern — the path of creation.
The oak is the thunder-god's tree across all IE traditions — lightning strikes the tallest tree.

The Monad is approached through prayer, sacrifice, and ritual — the living relationship between devotee and divine.
The Monad preserves and transmits the sacred knowledge of Gnostic.

facets.facet_name: Kvasir — The Wisest Being, Killed to Make the Mead of Poetry
facets.core_claim: Kvasir was made from the gods' mingled saliva — the wisest being, killed by dwarves to brew the Mead of Poetry.

facets.facet_name: The Original Völva — Giver of Seidr to the Gods
facets.core_claim: Freya is the prototypical völva (seeress/shaman) — she taught seidr to Odin and presides over all shamanic practice.

facets.facet_name: She Sings the Prophecy of the World's End
facets.core_claim: After defeating Fomorians, the Morrigan sings a great prophecy — the Irish parallel of Voluspa.

facets.facet_name: The Djed Pillar — His Spine, Symbol of Stability
facets.core_claim: The djed pillar is Osiris's backbone — raised each year in a ceremony of cosmic stability.

facets.facet_name: Her Wings Shelter and Protect All Who Seek Refuge
facets.core_claim: Isis spreads her great wings to shelter the dead and the living — the supreme Egyptian protective gesture.

facets.facet_name: Ra Has Existed for Millions of Years — The Eternal Sun
facets.core_claim: Ra is the sun that has existed for "millions and millions of years" — divine eternality expressed through cosmic time.

facets.facet_name: He Won Five Days from the Moon for the Gods to Be Born
facets.core_claim: Thoth gambled with the Moon and won 1/72 of each day — creating the five epagomenal days in which Osiris, Isis, Set, Nephthys, and Horus were born.

facets.facet_name: She Planted the Huluppu Tree — Her Throne and Bed
facets.core_claim: Inanna planted the huluppu tree to make her throne and bed — Gilgamesh cleared it of its divine inhabitants.

facets.facet_name: He and Ninhursag Made Flawed Humans from Clay in a Contest
facets.core_claim: Enki and Ninhursag competed to make beings from clay — creating flawed beings they then gave functions to.

facets.facet_name: The Akitu Festival — Cosmic Re-enactment of Creation
facets.core_claim: The Babylonian New Year festival re-enacted Marduk's defeat of Tiamat — the cosmos renewed each spring.
Shiva dwells on Mount Kailash — the sacred mountain that is both a physical place and the center of the cosmos.

facets.facet_name: Standing on Shiva's Prone Body — Activity Over Inertia
facets.core_claim: Kali stands on Shiva's corpse-like body — without her Shakti, Shiva is inert. She IS his energy.

facets.facet_name: In Vrindavana — The Divine Lover Playing the Flute
facets.core_claim: Krishna's childhood and youth in Vrindavana is the Hindu model of divine play (lila) — the cosmos as God's sport.

facets.facet_name: The Parinirvana — Final Passing at Kushinagar
facets.core_claim: The Buddha died at 80, surrounded by his monks — his last words: "Decay is inherent in all conditioned things. Strive on with diligence."

facets.facet_name: Vowed Not to Enter Nirvana Until All Beings Are Liberated
facets.core_claim: Avalokitesvara made the bodhisattva vow to remain in samsara until every being is free — cosmic compassion as eternal commitment.

facets.facet_name: The 18th Vow — Even Ten Sincere Callings Are Enough
facets.core_claim: Amitabha's 18th vow: beings who sincerely call his name even ten times will be reborn in his Pure Land — the most radical statement of Buddhist grace.

facets.facet_name: Zeus Xenos — Protector of Guests and Strangers
facets.core_claim: Zeus protects xenia (guest-friendship) — one of the oldest and most sacred obligations in Greek society.

facets.facet_name: Guide of the Dead to the Underworld
facets.core_claim: Hermes guides the souls of the dead to Hades — the divine escort between the living and dead worlds.

facets.facet_name: Born from the Sea Foam Around Uranos's Severed Genitals
facets.core_claim: Aphrodite rises from the sea foam generated by Uranos's castration — beauty emerges from cosmic violence.

facets.facet_name: Even Zeus and Hera Cannot Resist Her Power
facets.core_claim: Aphrodite's power is irresistible to all the gods — not a soft power but a cosmic force that overrides divine will.

facets.facet_name: She Stands at the Three-Way Crossroads
facets.core_claim: Hekate's triformis stands where three roads meet — the sacred liminal space that belongs to no single direction.

facets.facet_name: She Holds Two Torches — Illuminating the Underworld
facets.core_claim: Hekate's torches light the path through darkness — the divine illumination of the underworld.

facets.facet_name: Sacred Pigs at Eleusis — the Death of the Corn
facets.core_claim: The Eleusinian Mysteries began with the slaughter of pigs — the pig's burial and resurrection mirrors the grain.

facets.facet_name: As Kore the Maiden — Before the Descent
facets.core_claim: Persephone was Kore (the Maiden) before her descent — youth and innocence as a divine phase.

facets.facet_name: Twice Born — From Semele and from Zeus's Thigh
facets.core_claim: Dionysus was born twice — first from Semele, killed by Zeus's glory, then saved by being sewn into Zeus's thigh.

facets.facet_name: The Thyrsus — Fennel Staff Tipped with a Pine Cone
facets.core_claim: The thyrsus is Dionysus's sacred staff — the hidden fire within the reed (ecstasy dormant in ordinary reality).

facets.facet_name: Bound to a Rock — Liver Eaten Daily
facets.core_claim: Prometheus suffers eternally for humanity's sake — the divine rebel whose punishment IS his greatest gift (proof that defiance is possible).
Lugh's long arm reaches across all distance — the sun's rays as his arm extending everywhere.

facets.facet_name: His Cauldron Never Empties — Eternal Abundance
facets.core_claim: The Dagda's cauldron is one of the four treasures of the Tuatha De Danann — it feeds all who come to it.
Dian Cecht made Nuada a silver arm after his loss in battle — the divine craftsman restoring the king's integrity.

facets.facet_name: The Washer at the Ford — Omen of Death
facets.core_claim: The Morrigan sometimes appears as the Washer at the Ford, cleaning the armor of those about to die in battle.

facets.facet_name: The Gae Bolg — Death-Spear of the Sea God
facets.core_claim: The Gae Bolg is Cu Chulainn's terrible weapon — once thrown, it fills every cavity of the opponent's body.

facets.facet_name: Holds the Torque and the Serpent — Wealth and Earth Power
facets.core_claim: Cernunnos holds a torque (divine authority) and a serpent with a ram's head (earth power) — the two symbols of his domain.

facets.facet_name: Her Feast of Imbolc — Spring's First Breath
facets.core_claim: Imbolc (February 1) celebrates Brigid and the first stirring of spring — her sacred flame marks winter's end.

facets.facet_name: He Created the Kantele — Finnish Harp
facets.core_claim: Vainamoinen made the first kantele from the jawbone of a giant pike — music born from death transformed.
Inktomi created language — the gift that makes human community possible, from the trickster who also deceives with it.

basis: Both declare 'I am God and there is no other' (Isaiah 45:5, Apocryphon of John II,1)
Both demand exclusive worship and blood sacrifice
Both are jealous, punitive, lawgiving
Gnostic primary texts explicitly identify them
primary_text_evidence: Marcion's Antitheses (lost; preserved in Tertullian Adv. Marc.)
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: The identification is a Gnostic interpretive move; mainstream Jewish and Christian theology rejects it.

basis: Both descend into the underworld
Both are connected to fertility cycles
Both involve a substitution-and-return arrangement
Both are self-sacrifice on a tree/cross
Both are pierced by a spear
Both involve sacrifice 'of myself to myself' (Hávamál) / 'God offering God' (Trinitarian theology)
Both result in acquisition of cosmic knowledge or redemption
Both are trickster figures who benefit humanity
Both are punished by being bound
Both will be released at the cosmic crisis
Both bring fire/transformation
Both descend/come to humanity with civilizing gifts
Both oppose the dominant divine order
Both are exiled or bound for their action
Within Hindu cosmology, Shiva and Kali are consorts embodying the same destroyer-principle
Both dance the cosmic dance of dissolution
Both are paradoxically generative through destruction
Both betray and kill a brother-figure (Osiris, Baldr)
Both are subsequently bound/imprisoned
Both represent disorder against cosmic order
Both transmit highest wisdom
Both are associated with silence and direct understanding
Both are gurus to aged sages
Both bridge tiers (cross-tier teachers)
Greek Zeus Pater is the direct phonological reflex of PIE *Dyḗus Ph₂tḗr
Sky-father function is preserved unchanged
Both preside over a divine council
Both wield the thunderbolt (function inherited via storm-god overlay)
primary_text_evidence: Mycenaean Linear B di-we (dative of Zeus, c. 1400 BCE)
Reconstructed PIE form via Vedic Dyaus Pita, Latin Iūpiter, Norse Týr
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: The linguistic descent is consensus among Indo-Europeanists. Whether the full religious-functional descent is equally secure is debated for individual features.

basis: Both are sky-father / council-head of a divine pantheon
Both are addressed as 'Father' of the council
El and *Dyḗus Ph₂tḗr are NOT linguistically cognate — Semitic vs. Indo-European
The functional parallel is structural, not genetic
primary_text_evidence: Ugaritic Baal Cycle (El as council head)
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: Important not to confuse structural parallel with genetic descent. Semitic El and IE *Dyḗus Ph₂tḗr both occupy the sky-father slot, but the names are from entirely different language families.

basis: Both wield the thunder-weapon (PIE *perkʷu- 'oak' / *per- 'to strike')
Both slay a serpent who has dammed the waters
Vedic Parjanya (storm-god, distinct from Indra) is the more direct *Perkʷunos cognate; Indra inherits the *function*
primary_text_evidence: Reconstructed *Perkʷunos via Slavic Perun, Baltic Perkūnas, Vedic Parjanya
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: Indra's name is not directly cognate with *Perkʷunos. The structural and mythological descent is clear; the linguistic lineage runs partly through Parjanya.

basis: Both are the storm-god slaying the serpent-of-the-waters
Both release the cosmic rivers thereby
Same myth in Greek (Zeus vs. Typhon), Hittite (Tarhunna vs. Illuyanka), Iranian (Thraētaona vs. Aži Dahāka), Norse (Thor vs. Jörmungandr)
Calvert Watkins, 'How to Kill a Dragon' (1995), traces the inherited formulae
primary_text_evidence: Reconstructed PIE storm-myth via cognate narratives

basis: Both slay the primordial water-serpent
Both establish cosmic order thereby
Marduk slays Tiamat (the salt waters); Indra slays Vritra (who has trapped the waters)
The chaoskampf is one of the most widespread Bronze Age Near Eastern / Indo-European narratives
canonical_parallels.source_tradition: Comparative ancient Near Eastern / IE mythology

basis: Trito is the mortal warrior; *Perkʷunos is the divine storm-god
In some daughter traditions (Norse Thor) the two functions merge
In others (Vedic) they are distinguished: Indra is the storm-king; Trita Āptya is the third warrior
Bruce Lincoln, 'Death, War, and Sacrifice' (1991): the IE warrior-myth has both a divine and a human pole
Both are former supreme gods reduced in status
Both are humbled by encounters with a higher principle
Indra is humbled by Krishna (Govardhana), Vishnu, and various sages
Yaldabaoth is exposed as not-the-true-Most-High by Sophia and Christ-Aeon
The parallel is structural, not historical
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: Mainstream Hindu theology does not frame Indra's demotion in demiurgic terms. The parallel is a structural observation, not a Hindu doctrine.

basis: Both are the all-seeing enforcer of the oath/covenant
Both bind the false-swearer
Both originated in older sky-god strata and were preserved as moral-legal sovereigns
Georges Dumézil's trifunctional analysis identifies Varuna as the 'magical-sovereign' parallel to the Hebrew covenantal god
Mitra and Varuna are systematically paired in Vedic religion
Mitra is the friendly contract; Varuna is the dread oath
They are addressed together (Mitra-Varuna) more than separately in the Rigveda
Both center fire as the link between human and divine
Prometheus: fire is *brought* to humans against the high god's will (transgressive)
Agni: fire is the natural messenger between worlds (sanctioned)
The same element, framed inversely — transgressive in Greek myth, sanctioned in Vedic ritual
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: The structural inversion is itself interesting: the same element receives opposite mythological framing in two IE traditions.

basis: Vedic Agni is the direct phonological reflex of PIE *h₁n̥gʷnis 'fire'
Cognate set: Sanskrit agni, Latin ignis, Lithuanian ugnis, Slavic ogon', Hittite Agniš
Vedic preserves the deity-status most clearly
Both are Hindu deities of ending — but at different scales
Yama: individual death and post-mortem judgment
Shiva: cosmic dissolution at the end of yugas
Functionally complementary within the same tradition
Both are feminine principles whose fierce or fallen aspect conceals the ultimate liberative function
Both demand the destruction of ego to reveal the true Self
Kali in Tantric theology, Sophia in Gnostic theology, occupy structurally parallel roles
Both prophesied to return at the end of the current age
Both restore a former golden age (Satya Yuga / Toltec golden age)
Both ride/arrive with distinctive iconography (white horse / from the east)
The cluster also includes the returning Christ, the Mahdi, Maitreya, Saoshyant, the once-and-future Arthur
Both occupy the 'true Most High' / preserver-of-order slot
Both are addressed as Father / Supreme
Both have a council/court of subordinate divine beings (lokapalas / bene ha-elohim)
Both are angelic/divine beings who exceed their station
Both bring about a corrupted world via their transgression
Enochic Watchers are bound in the abyss; Yaldabaoth is exposed as not-the-Most-High
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: The Enochic Watchers and the Gnostic Archons are historically related streams of late Second Temple thought, though not identical.

basis: Both bring forbidden knowledge from divine realm to humanity
Both are punished by being bound (Prometheus to the Caucasus, Watchers in the abyss)
The valence is opposite: Greek tradition celebrates Prometheus; Enochic tradition condemns the Watchers
The structural parallel is exact; the moral framing inverts
primary_text_evidence: Aeschylus, Prometheus Bound (Prometheus enumerates gifts to humanity)

basis: Both involve divine beings acting beyond their ordained scope
Sophia produces Yaldabaoth without her consort, against the Pleromatic order
The Watchers act without divine sanction in descending and teaching
Both result in cosmic disorder requiring redemption
Both are divine/angelic figures bound after transgression
Both await release at a final reckoning (Day of Judgment / Ragnarök)
Both are associated with crafty/intelligent rebellion against the divine order
Both have a substance dripped on them in some traditions (serpent venom on Loki; mythological elaborations on Azazel)
Both populate or define a previous world-age that ends in catastrophe
In Aztec cosmology, previous suns were destroyed when their populations grew corrupt
In Enochic tradition, the Nephilim's corruption ends the pre-Flood world
The 'previous corrupt age destroyed by reset' motif
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: The parallel is at the level of cosmic-cycle structure, not specific narrative.

basis: Metatron is called 'lesser YHWH' and sits at God's right hand
El Elyon's council includes 'sons of God' (bene elohim)
Metatron represents the limit-case elevation of a created being into the divine council
Parallels with Christology — the human-divine intermediary
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: Metatron's status was controversial in rabbinic Judaism; the parallel is structural, not endorsed by mainstream tradition.

basis: Trito and his daughter-traditions (Heracles, Thor) slay the giant/monster
The Nephilim are the giants who must be slain — or who must be drowned
Across IE and Near Eastern traditions, the pre-current-age giants (Titans, Jotnar, Daityas, Nephilim, Rephaim) are the antagonists of the founding warrior-myth
The structural complement: every dragon-slayer needs a dragon; every Heracles needs a Geryon; every Flood needs Nephilim
Both names descend from PIE *deh₂nu- 'flowing waters / river'
Both are mother-figures of a divine race named after them (Tuath Dé Danann / Dānavas)
The same root names the rivers Danube, Don, Dnieper, Dniester, Donets
Mallory & Adams, Encyclopedia of Indo-European Culture; Calvert Watkins, How to Kill a Dragon
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: The linguistic descent is solid; the question of whether the two goddesses share more than a name (i.e. whether their full mythological complex descends from a single PIE figure) is debated. Most IE-ists accept the cognate; some prefer to treat the Irish and Vedic figures as parallel developments from a common name rather than a fully shared figure.

basis: Both are mothers of a divine race named after them
Tuath Dé Danann ('Peoples of the Goddess Danu') and Dānavas ('Descendants of Dānu') are the same naming pattern
Diti and Danu are sister-wives of Kashyapa
Both mother contending asura-clans (Daityas / Dānavas)
Their pairing with Aditi (mother of Devas) forms the Vedic triadic structure of cosmic mothers
Both bear the divine council (Ādityas / sons of El)
Both are consorts of the council-head (Kashyapa-as-prajapati / El)
Both are mothers-of-the-bright-gods, with countable son-lists
Both are progenitor-fathers of contending divine lineages
Both are withdrawn from active combat (younger gods do the fighting)
Both grant kingship / dynastic legitimacy
Both are primordial water-chaos serpent-figures
Both are slain by a storm-god (Indra / Baal) to establish cosmic order
Both are 'the encloser / withholder' against the life-giving rain-god
Calvert Watkins, How to Kill a Dragon (1995), establishes the formula *gʷhen- 'slay' + ogʷhi- 'serpent' across the IE family and into West Semitic borrowings
Both are pre-cultivated-order chaos powers slain by the bright-god hero
Both produce cosmic abundance upon their death (Vṛtra: the waters; Balor: end of Fomorian tyranny)
The PIE Trito-myth: third warrior with the storm-god slays a three-headed serpent and recovers stolen cattle/waters
Baal slaying Yam and recovering the cosmic kingship is the West Semitic version
Indra-Vṛtra is the Vedic; Thor-Jörmungandr the Norse; Herakles-Geryon the Greek
Both are older monstrous races defeated by the cultivated-order gods
Both are named in relation to the goddess Danu (Dānavas = children of Vedic Dānu; Tuath Dé Danann are children of Irish Danu)
Note however the inversion: the Vedic Dānavas are the LOSERS while the Irish Tuath Dé are the WINNERS — same root mother-name, opposite alignment
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: David Frawley's argument that the Dānavas and Tuath Dé share both name and substance — and that the European peoples are 'the children of Danu' in a directly-historical sense — is contested. Mainstream IE-ists accept the linguistic cognate but are wary of the historical-migration claim.

basis: Both are giant warrior-races of the older order
Both have moral ambivalence (Daityas include Prahlada and Bali; Rephaim are simultaneously feared and the deified-dead ancestors)
Both must be defeated for the cultivated-order to be established (Indra / Joshua)
Both are giant races of divine-and-mortal mixed parentage
Dānavas: sons of Kashyapa-rishi (semi-divine prajapati) and Dānu (water-goddess)
Nephilim: sons of the bnei elohim and the daughters of men
Both are amānuṣa / non-fully-human and require divine intervention to subdue
Both are monstrous giant races defeated by the gods/heroes of the cultivated order
Both are described in terms of physical deformity (Fomorians: one-eyed/one-armed; Nephilim: 'men of renown' of unnatural stature)
Both are of mixed divine/chthonic parentage
Deuteronomy 2:11 explicitly: 'They also are accounted Rephaim, like the Anakim'
Numbers 13:33: the Anakim 'come from the Nephilim'
Same geographic complex (Hebron / Bashan / Mt Hermon)
Numbers 13:33: 'And there we saw the Nephilim, the sons of Anak, who come from the Nephilim'
The text places Anakim in the direct genealogical line of the Nephilim
Egyptian Iy-ʿanaq (Execration Texts, 19th–18th c. BCE) preserves the ʿayin consonant matching Hebrew ʿănāq
Geography matches: both denote enemies in southern Canaan / Hebron region
Three rulers with Semitic names in the Egyptian texts mirror the three sons of Anak (Sheshai, Ahiman, Talmai) at Hebron
W.F. Albright, Journal of the Palestine Oriental Society 8 (1928); G. Mazar (1949)
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: The identification is widely accepted but not universal. Encyclopedia.com notes the three Hebrew sons-of-Anak names (Ahiman, Sheshai, Talmai) are Hurrian rather than Semitic in their affinities, complicating a direct equation.

basis: Ugaritic rpum cult is centered on Bashan / Mt Hermon
Biblical Og is 'king of Bashan' and 'last of the Rephaim'
1 Enoch 6 places the Watchers' descent on Mt Hermon
Three traditions independently locate giant / divine-rebel / ancestral-warrior cult in the same geography
Talmud Niddah 61a explicitly makes Og the grandson of Shemhazai the fallen Watcher
Connects the post-flood Rephaim-line directly to the pre-flood Watcher-line
Same root word rpʾ in both languages
Same dual register: giants AND shades-of-dead
Hebrew inherits the polysemy directly from West Semitic substrate
Both die, descend, and return
Both are associated with seasonal/cosmic renewal
Baal cycle structurally precedes Christ by 1000+ years and was geographically continuous with Iron Age Israel
Frazer, Smith, Mettinger on dying-and-rising gods
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: Mettinger (2001) defends the dying-and-rising-god category for Baal, Adonis, and Osiris; Tryggve Mettinger argues these are genuine antecedents to the Christian pattern, while other scholars (Smith, Frazer-critics) have argued the category is overdrawn. The geographic and chronological continuity between Baal religion and Israelite religion is uncontested.

basis: Both die/descend in summer drought / winter; both return for the rains/spring
Both have a sister/lover figure (Anat / Demeter) whose grief drives the cosmic search
Same divine name (ʾIlu / El)
Same divine-council role
Same allotting-the-nations function (Ugaritic Baal Cycle parallels Deut 32:8-9)
Mark S. Smith, The Origins of Biblical Monotheism (2001); F.M. Cross, Canaanite Myth and Hebrew Epic
RV 10.90 (Puruṣa Sūkta) is the most explicit attestation of the reconstructed PIE *Manu / *Yemo cosmogony
Bruce Lincoln, 'The Indo-European Myth of Creation' (History of Religions 15, 1975)
Mallory & Adams, Encyclopedia of IE Culture
Vedic Yama is the direct Sanskrit reflex of PIE *Yemo
Both are first to die, hence first king of the dead
Avestan Yima preserves the same complex (with the additional 'first king of golden age' aspect)
Both master every art/craft
Both are cross-tier bridge-figures (mortal/divine, mundane/wisdom)
Both are descended cultural-hero / teacher types
Both are youthful, multi-talented, light-associated bringers of wisdom
Both decisively defeat an entrenched tyrant-figure
Both wade in blood on the battlefield
Both combine erotic and martial functions in a single figure
Both test and accompany the hero
Both are triple/many-form war and fate goddesses
Both are simultaneously feared and venerated as cosmic-mother
Both are impersonal principles above the named gods
Both are the source of order against which the gods are measured
Vedic Ṛta (PIE *h₂er-) is cognate with Avestan aša and conceptually parallel to Egyptian ma'at
Both are 'all-father' figures
Dagda's epithet Ollathair ('all-father') matches the function of *Dyḗus Ph₂tḗr exactly
Both grant kingship, abundance, and dynastic continuity
Both are wisdom-holding all-fathers of the divine race
Both have abundance-instrument (Dagda's cauldron / Odin's mead of poetry)
Both are withdrawn sky-father high gods who do not directly create the world
Both act through a son or active subordinate (Wallungunder / Baal)
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: Comparative parallels between Aboriginal cosmology and Mediterranean/Near Eastern divine-council structures should be drawn with caution; the underlying religious frameworks are profoundly different and the Aboriginal tradition has its own integrity.

basis: Wallungunder is the active world-creator son of the withdrawn Idjair
Structurally parallel to Baal-as-active under withdrawn El
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: Functional parallel only; no historical connection.

basis: Both control rain, lightning, and storm
Both are the active fertility-bringer of an arid landscape
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: Functional parallel only; the Worrorra worldview is not derivable from West Semitic categories.

basis: Both wield lightning
Both are primary rain-bringers
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: Functional parallel.

basis: Both are primordial water-associated creator-beings
Both are associated with deep pools and life-giving waters
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: The Rainbow Serpent is not structurally identical to the Vedic Dānu — Dānu is a mother-goddess, Wunggurr a serpentine creator-being. Functional overlap only.

basis: Both bring transformative technology to humanity (metallurgy and weapons / fire and crafts)
Both are punished by binding (Azazel in Dudael; Prometheus on the Caucasus)
Same structural slot with inverted moral valence
Stroumsa, Another Seed: Studies in Gnostic Mythology (1984) develops this parallel at length
Both are cosmic-rebel figures bound by the gods until the end-time
Both will be released at the eschatological moment (Loki at Ragnarök; the Watchers at the great judgment)
Both are imprisoned in a pit-realm (Dudael / Tartarus)
Both await final judgment
Book of Giants 4Q530 explicitly establishes the parentage
Talmud Niddah 61a: Og is grandson of Shemhazai through Ahijah
Connects the antediluvian Watcher tradition directly to the post-flood Rephaim
Book of Giants 4Q530 frag 7 and 4Q203 frag 8 explicitly establish the parentage
Both Gilgamesh and Hobabish/Humbaba are imported from the Akkadian Gilgamesh epic into the Aramaic Book of Giants
Direct evidence of Mesopotamian-to-Jewish transmission of heroic material
Both are the elder, primordial divine race against which the cultivated-order gods define themselves
Both have a primordial body-becomes-cosmos figure (no titanic figure in Greek = unclear; in Norse, Ymir)
Both end up defeated/imprisoned (Tartarus / Jötunheim with Ragnarök revenge)
Both are the elder divine race overthrown by the younger gods (Olympians / Devas)
Both include figures of moral ambivalence rather than pure evil
Both are the dominant beings of a prior cosmic age, destroyed when that age ended
Both are credited with building megalithic structures in some traditions
Both are mighty pre-flood beings
Both have divine parentage and are destroyed for that very mixing
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: The Titans are not specifically pre-flood in Greek tradition; the parallel is structural, not chronological.

basis: Both are the elder monstrous race defeated by the gods of the cultivated order
Both are associated with sea/cold/wild edges
Both include figures (Loki / Bres) who marry into the gods
Both involve cataclysmic destruction tied to the elder race
Inverted valence: in Norse the giants destroy the gods at the end; in Aztec the giants are destroyed at the end of the prior age
Both are Indigenous American giant traditions
Both describe giants ultimately destroyed by allied human tribes / cosmic catastrophe
Both have been the subject of contested archaeology
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: Independent traditions from different culture-areas; no claim of common origin.

basis: Both are red-haired or strikingly-featured giant peoples
Both wage war against the people who become the storytellers (Paiute / Israelites)
Both are ultimately destroyed by the storytellers' victory
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: Functional parallel; no historical connection. The Si-Te-Cah tradition is also itself contested between its giant-version and its ordinary-humans-hostile-tribe version.

basis: Both are the PIE *Yemo cosmogony — first being whose dismembered body becomes the world
Bruce Lincoln (1975), Mallory & Adams Encyclopedia of IE Culture
Old Norse Ymir, Vedic Yama, Avestan Yima all descend from PIE *Yemo 'twin'
All three preserve the first-mortal / king-of-the-dead aspect
Both involve the body of a prior being becoming the substance of present life
Ymir's body becomes the cosmos; the Quinametzin's bones become humanity
Both appear at the end of the cosmic age with a flaming sword
Both destroy the existing order to inaugurate (Norse: end; Vedic: renewal) the next
Calvert Watkins discusses the IE shared eschatological-warrior structure
Both ride a white horse (Kalki on Devadatta; the rider of Rev 19 on a white horse)
Both wield a sword to destroy the wicked at the end of the age
Both inaugurate the new age
Both are giant-clan traditions with extra-textual attestation (Egyptian Execration Texts / Spanish Conquistador chronicler accounts)
Both are attested by hostile outside observers who used the term 'giants' for the people they were curse-cursing / conquering
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: Independent traditions from different millennia and culture-areas.

basis: Both involve a prior race or generation aspiring to reach the gods through building
Both are punished by divine intervention
Spanish chroniclers immediately drew this parallel
Nimrod founds Erech (Uruk) — Gilgamesh's city
Some traditions identify the two figures or place them in the same generation
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: The identification is interpretive; some early commentators (especially in rabbinic and Islamic tradition) made it, but the Hebrew Bible does not.

basis: Both feature interbreeding between two ontological classes (Æsir/jötunn; bnei-elohim/daughters-of-men)
Both produce mixed offspring
Opposite valence: Norse treats this as norm; Genesis 6 treats it as cataclysm
Both reflect the principle that the gods and the elder race share parentage
Norse: Æsir descend from Bestla the jötunn; Vedic: Devas, Daityas, and Danavas all descend from Kashyapa
Both are elder tyrants overthrown by a younger relative (Zeus / Lugh)
Both are slain or imprisoned by their own grandsons
Both are slain by the younger storm-god / king-by-combat (Zeus / Indra)
Both belong to the elder generation of cosmic powers
Both involve members of the defeated/prior generation holding up the sky
The four Quinametzin sky-bearers (Cuauhtemoc, Izcoalt, Izcaqlli, Tenexuche) parallel Atlas's role
Both are mothers of the younger generation of gods who overthrow the elder
Both bear named, countable son-lists
Both are consort of the patriarch and mother of the divine council
Rhea bears the six elder Olympians; Asherah the seventy sons of El
Both are specific named individuals described as giants in their respective tradition's historical narrative
Both are killed/defeated by the people the narrative valorizes (David / Spanish forces with Aztec allies)
Both involve specific weight-of-weapon details (Goliath's spear; Tzilacatzin's watermelon-sized rocks)
canonical_parallels.scholarly_caveat: The Tzilacatzin tradition is preserved in Spanish chronicles within a generation of his life; the Goliath narrative is older and more layered.

basis: Both are named in 1 Enoch 8:3 as Watcher chiefs who taught celestial signs
Kokabiel is a specific instance of the Watcher-teachers-of-forbidden-arts class
Sariel teaches lunar courses; Samsiel teaches solar signs — complementary halves of forbidden celestial knowledge
PIE *Perkwunos direct descendants
both storm-gods who slay chthonic serpent
PIE *Perkwunos vs *Ngwhi (serpent) dyad
Perun/Veles :: Indra/Vritra
canonical_parallels.scholarly_note: Calvert Watkins, How to Kill a Dragon (Oxford 1995)

basis: Dragon-slayer formula
eight-headed serpent slain by storm/warrior figure
canonical_parallels.scholarly_note: Structural parallel; Japanese tradition not PIE but shares the formula

basis: Chaoskampf — storm deity slays primordial serpent/dragon
Both die and return
both judge the dead
Osiris iconography influenced early Christian art in Egypt
canonical_parallels.scholarly_note: Documented cultural contact via Isis mystery cult in Hellenistic period

basis: Both dismembered and resurrected
both wine/vegetation associations
Plutarch discusses parallel
canonical_parallels.scholarly_note: Plutarch, On Isis and Osiris — documents ancient identification

basis: Both descend to underworld
both return
both linked to seasonal cycle
canonical_parallels.scholarly_note: Structural parallel; documented transmission debated

basis: Both are future returning saviors awaiting the right moment to descend
both close the current degraded age
